{
  "file_path": "/workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp",
  "new_string": "ReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                             std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                             std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),\n      is_valid_(true) {\n  // The caller (BPM) should have already acquired the read lock before calling this constructor\n  // The guard takes ownership of the lock\n}",
  "old_string": "ReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                             std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                             std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)) {\n  UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\");\n}",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp",
  "newString": "ReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                             std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                             std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),\n      is_valid_(true) {\n  // The caller (BPM) should have already acquired the read lock before calling this constructor\n  // The guard takes ownership of the lock\n}",
  "oldString": "ReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                             std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                             std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)) {\n  UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\");\n}",
  "originalFile": "//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// page_guard.cpp\n//\n// Identification: src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#include \"storage/page/page_guard.h\"\n#include <memory>\n#include \"buffer/arc_replacer.h\"\n#include \"common/macros.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\n/**\n * @brief The only constructor for an RAII `ReadPageGuard` that creates a valid guard.\n *\n * Note that only the buffer pool manager is allowed to call this constructor.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param frame A shared pointer to the frame that holds the page we want to protect.\n * @param replacer A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's replacer.\n * @param bpm_latch A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's latch.\n * @param disk_scheduler A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's disk scheduler.\n */\nReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                             std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                             std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)) {\n  UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\");\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move constructor for `ReadPageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n */\nReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept {}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move assignment operator for `ReadPageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each, and for the current object, make sure you release any resources it might be\n * holding on to.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n * @return ReadPageGuard& The newly valid `ReadPageGuard`.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::operator=(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept -> ReadPageGuard & { return *this; }\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets the page ID of the page this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::GetPageId() const -> page_id_t {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return page_id_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a `const` pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::GetData() const -> const char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return frame_->GetData();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Returns whether the page is dirty (modified but not flushed to the disk).\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::IsDirty() const -> bool {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return frame_->is_dirty_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes this page's data safely to disk.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid ReadPageGuard::Flush() { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Manually drops a valid `ReadPageGuard`'s data. If this guard is invalid, this function does nothing.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * Make sure you don't double free! Also, think **very** **VERY** carefully about what resources you own and the order\n * in which you release those resources. If you get the ordering wrong, you will very likely fail one of the later\n * Gradescope tests. You may also want to take the buffer pool manager's latch in a very specific scenario...\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid ReadPageGuard::Drop() { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/** @brief The destructor for `ReadPageGuard`. This destructor simply calls `Drop()`. */\nReadPageGuard::~ReadPageGuard() { Drop(); }\n\n/**********************************************************************************************************************/\n/**********************************************************************************************************************/\n/**********************************************************************************************************************/\n\n/**\n * @brief The only constructor for an RAII `WritePageGuard` that creates a valid guard.\n *\n * Note that only the buffer pool manager is allowed to call this constructor.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to write to.\n * @param frame A shared pointer to the frame that holds the page we want to protect.\n * @param replacer A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's replacer.\n * @param bpm_latch A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's latch.\n * @param disk_scheduler A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's disk scheduler.\n */\nWritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                               std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                               std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)) {\n  UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\");\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move constructor for `WritePageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n */\nWritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(WritePageGuard &&that) noexcept {}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move assignment operator for `WritePageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each, and for the current object, make sure you release any resources it might be\n * holding on to.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n * @return WritePageGuard& The newly valid `WritePageGuard`.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::operator=(WritePageGuard &&that) noexcept -> WritePageGuard & { return *this; }\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets the page ID of the page this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetPageId() const -> page_id_t {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return page_id_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a `const` pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetData() const -> const char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return frame_->GetData();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a mutable pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetDataMut() -> char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return frame_->GetDataMut();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Returns whether the page is dirty (modified but not flushed to the disk).\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::IsDirty() const -> bool {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return frame_->is_dirty_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes this page's data safely to disk.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid WritePageGuard::Flush() { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Manually drops a valid `WritePageGuard`'s data. If this guard is invalid, this function does nothing.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * Make sure you don't double free! Also, think **very** **VERY** carefully about what resources you own and the order\n * in which you release those resources. If you get the ordering wrong, you will very likely fail one of the later\n * Gradescope tests. You may also want to take the buffer pool manager's latch in a very specific scenario...\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid WritePageGuard::Drop() { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/** @brief The destructor for `WritePageGuard`. This destructor simply calls `Drop()`. */\nWritePageGuard::~WritePageGuard() { Drop(); }\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n",

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp",
  "new_string": "ReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept\n    : page_id_(that.page_id_),\n      frame_(std::move(that.frame_)),\n      replacer_(std::move(that.replacer_)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(that.bpm_latch_)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(that.disk_scheduler_)),\n      is_valid_(that.is_valid_) {\n  // Invalidate the moved-from guard\n  that.is_valid_ = false;\n}",

}

  "originalFile": "//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// page_guard.cpp\n//\n// Identification: src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#include \"storage/page/page_guard.h\"\n#include <memory>\n#include \"buffer/arc_replacer.h\"\n#include \"common/macros.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\n/**\n * @brief The only constructor for an RAII `ReadPageGuard` that creates a valid guard.\n *\n * Note that only the buffer pool manager is allowed to call this constructor.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param frame A shared pointer to the frame that holds the page we want to protect.\n * @param replacer A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's replacer.\n * @param bpm_latch A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's latch.\n * @param disk_scheduler A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's disk scheduler.\n */\nReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                             std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                             std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),\n      is_valid_(true) {\n  // The caller (BPM) should have already acquired the read lock before calling this constructor\n  // The guard takes ownership of the lock\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move constructor for `ReadPageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n */\nReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept {}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move assignment operator for `ReadPageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each, and for the current object, make sure you release any resources it might be\n * holding on to.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n * @return ReadPageGuard& The newly valid `ReadPageGuard`.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::operator=(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept -> ReadPageGuard & { return *this; }\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets the page ID of the page this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::GetPageId() const -> page_id_t {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return page_id_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a `const` pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::GetData() const -> const char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return frame_->GetData();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Returns whether the page is dirty (modified but not flushed to the disk).\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::IsDirty() const -> bool {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return frame_->is_dirty_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes this page's data safely to disk.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid ReadPageGuard::Flush() { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Manually drops a valid `ReadPageGuard`'s data. If this guard is invalid, this function does nothing.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * Make sure you don't double free! Also, think **very** **VERY** carefully about what resources you own and the order\n * in which you release those resources. If you get the ordering wrong, you will very likely fail one of the later\n * Gradescope tests. You may also want to take the buffer pool manager's latch in a very specific scenario...\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid ReadPageGuard::Drop() { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/** @brief The destructor for `ReadPageGuard`. This destructor simply calls `Drop()`. */\nReadPageGuard::~ReadPageGuard() { Drop(); }\n\n/**********************************************************************************************************************/\n/**********************************************************************************************************************/\n/**********************************************************************************************************************/\n\n/**\n * @brief The only constructor for an RAII `WritePageGuard` that creates a valid guard.\n *\n * Note that only the buffer pool manager is allowed to call this constructor.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to write to.\n * @param frame A shared pointer to the frame that holds the page we want to protect.\n * @param replacer A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's replacer.\n * @param bpm_latch A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's latch.\n * @param disk_scheduler A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's disk scheduler.\n */\nWritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                               std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                               std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)) {\n  UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\");\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move constructor for `WritePageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems!   "originalFile": "//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// page_guard.cpp\n//\n// Identification: src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#include \"storage/page/page_guard.h\"\n#include <memory>\n#include \"buffer/arc_replacer.h\"\n#include \"common/macros.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\n/**\n * @brief The only constructor for an RAII `ReadPageGuard` that creates a valid guard.\n *\n * Note that only the buffer pool manager is allowed to call this constructor.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param frame A shared pointer to the frame that holds the page we want to protect.\n * @param replacer A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's replacer.\n * @param bpm_latch A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's latch.\n * @param disk_scheduler A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's disk scheduler.\n */\nReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                             std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                             std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),\n      is_valid_(true) {\n  // The caller (BPM) should have already acquired the read lock before calling this constructor\n  // The guard takes ownership of the lock\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move constructor for `ReadPageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n */\nReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept\n    : page_id_(that.page_id_),\n      frame_(std::move(that.frame_)),\n      replacer_(std::move(that.replacer_)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(that.bpm_latch_)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(that.disk_scheduler_)),\n      is_valid_(that.is_valid_) {\n  // Invalidate the moved-from guard\n  that.is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move assignment operator for `ReadPageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each, and for the current object, make sure you release any resources it might be\n * holding on to.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n * @return ReadPageGuard& The newly valid `ReadPageGuard`.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::operator=(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept -> ReadPageGuard & { return *this; }\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets the page ID of the page this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::GetPageId() const -> page_id_t {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return page_id_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a `const` pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::GetData() const -> const char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return frame_->GetData();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Returns whether the page is dirty (modified but not flushed to the disk).\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::IsDirty() const -> bool {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return frame_->is_dirty_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes this page's data safely to disk.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid ReadPageGuard::Flush() { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Manually drops a valid `ReadPageGuard`'s data. Also, think **very** **VERY** carefully about what resources you own and the order\n * in which you release those resources. If you get the ordering wrong, you will very likely fail one of the later\n * Gradescope tests. You may also want to take the buffer pool manager's latch in a very specific scenario...\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid ReadPageGuard::Drop() { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/** @brief The destructor for `ReadPageGuard`. 59. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp",
  "new_string": "void ReadPageGuard::Flush() {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n\n  if (frame_->is_dirty_) {\n    // Write the page to disk\n    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n    DiskRequest request;\n    request.is_write_ = true;\n    request.data_ = frame_->GetDataMut();\n    request.page_id_ = page_id_;\n    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n    auto future = promise.get_future();\n    request.callback_ = std::move(promise);\n    requests.push_back(std::move(request));\n    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n    future.get();\n\n    frame_->is_dirty_ = false;\n  }\n}",
  "old_string": "void ReadPageGuard::Flush() { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }",
  "replace_all": false
}

{
  "filePath": "/workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp",

  "oldString": "void ReadPageGuard::Flush() { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }",
  "originalFile": "//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// page_guard.cpp\n//\n// Identification: src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#include \"storage/page/page_guard.h\"\n#include <memory>\n#include \"buffer/arc_replacer.h\"\n#include \"common/macros.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\n/**\n * @brief The only constructor for an RAII `ReadPageGuard` that creates a valid guard.\n *\n * Note that only the buffer pool manager is allowed to call this constructor.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param frame A shared pointer to the frame that holds the page we want to protect.\n * @param replacer A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's replacer.\n * @param bpm_latch A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's latch.\n * @param disk_scheduler A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's disk scheduler.\n */\nReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                             std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                             std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),\n      is_valid_(true) {\n  // The caller (BPM) should have already acquired the read lock before calling this constructor\n  // The guard takes ownership of the lock\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move constructor for `ReadPageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n */\nReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept\n    : page_id_(that.page_id_),\n      frame_(std::move(that.frame_)),\n      replacer_(std::move(that.replacer_)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(that.bpm_latch_)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(that.disk_scheduler_)),\n      is_valid_(that.is_valid_) {\n  // Invalidate the moved-from guard\n  that.is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move assignment operator for `ReadPageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each, and for the current object, make sure you release any resources it might be\n * holding on to.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n * @return ReadPageGuard& The newly valid `ReadPageGuard`.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::operator=(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept -> ReadPageGuard & {\n  if (this != &that) {\n    // First, drop any resources this guard might hold\n    Drop();\n\n    // Move the resources from 'that' to 'this'\n    page_id_ = that.page_id_;\n    frame_ = std::move(that.frame_);\n    replacer_ = std::move(that.replacer_);\n    bpm_latch_ = std::move(that.bpm_latch_);\n    disk_scheduler_ = std::move(that.disk_scheduler_);\n    is_valid_ = that.is_valid_;\n\n    // Invalidate the moved-from guard\n    that.is_valid_ = false;\n  }\n  return *this;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets the page ID of the page this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::GetPageId() const -> page_id_t {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return page_id_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a `const` pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::GetData() const -> const char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return frame_->GetData();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Returns whether the page is dirty (modified but not flushed to the disk).\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::IsDirty() const -> bool {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return frame_->is_dirty_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes this page's data safely to disk.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid ReadPageGuard::Flush() { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Manually drops a valid `ReadPageGuard`'s data. Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp",
  "new_string": "void ReadPageGuard::Drop() {\n  if (!is_valid_) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Unlock the shared lock on the frame\n  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock_shared();\n\n  // Decrement pin count and potentially set evictable\n  size_t old_pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1);\n  if (old_pin_count == 1) {\n    // This was the last pin, set the frame as evictable\n    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);\n    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);\n  }\n\n  // Invalidate the guard\n  is_valid_ = false;\n}",
  "old_string": "void ReadPageGuard::Drop() { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }",
  "replace_all": false
}

  "newString": "void ReadPageGuard::Drop() {\n  if (!is_valid_) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Unlock the shared lock on the frame\n  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock_shared();\n\n  // Decrement pin count and potentially set evictable\n  size_t old_pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1);\n  if (old_pin_count == 1) {\n    // This was the last pin, set the frame as evictable\n    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);\n    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);\n  }\n\n  // Invalidate the guard\n  is_valid_ = false;\n}",

For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n */\nReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept\n    : page_id_(that.page_id_),\n      frame_(std::move(that.frame_)),\n      replacer_(std::move(that.replacer_)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(that.bpm_latch_)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(that.disk_scheduler_)),\n      is_valid_(that.is_valid_) {\n  // Invalidate the moved-from guard\n  that.is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move assignment operator for `ReadPageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each, and for the current object, make sure you release any resources it might be\n * holding on to.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n * @return ReadPageGuard& The newly valid `ReadPageGuard`.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::operator=(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept -> ReadPageGuard & {\n  if (this != &that) {\n    // First, drop any resources this guard might hold\n    Drop();\n\n    // Move the resources from 'that' to 'this'\n    page_id_ = that.page_id_;\n    frame_ = std::move(that.frame_);\n    replacer_ = std::move(that.replacer_);\n    bpm_latch_ = std::move(that.bpm_latch_);\n    disk_scheduler_ = std::move(that.disk_scheduler_);\n    is_valid_ = that.is_valid_;\n\n    // Invalidate the moved-from guard\n    that.is_valid_ = false;\n  }\n  return *this;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets the page ID of the page this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto     "content": "}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes this page's data safely to disk.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid ReadPageGuard::Flush() {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n\n  if (frame_->is_dirty_) {\n    // Write the page to disk\n    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n    DiskRequest request;\n    request.is_write_ = true;\n    request.data_ = frame_->GetDataMut();\n    request.page_id_ = page_id_;\n    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n    auto future = promise.get_future();\n    request.callback_ = std::move(promise);\n    requests.push_back(std::move(request));\n    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n    future.get();\n\n    frame_->is_dirty_ = false;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Manually drops a valid `ReadPageGuard`'s data. If this guard is invalid, this function does nothing.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * Make sure you don't double free! Also, think **very** **VERY** carefully about what resources you own and the order\n * in which you release those resources. If you get the ordering wrong, you will very likely fail one of the later\n * Gradescope tests. You may also want to take the buffer pool manager's latch in a very specific scenario...\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid ReadPageGuard::Drop() {\n  if (!is_valid_) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Unlock the shared lock on the frame\n  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock_shared();\n\n  // Decrement pin count and potentially set evictable\n  size_t old_pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1);\n  if (old_pin_count == 1) {\n    // This was the last pin, set the frame as evictable\n    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);\n    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);\n  }\n\n  // Invalidate the guard\n  is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/** @brief The destructor for `ReadPageGuard`. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n */\nWritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(WritePageGuard &&that) noexcept {}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move assignment operator for `WritePageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp",
  "new_string": "WritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                               std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                               std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),\n      is_valid_(true) {\n  // The caller (BPM) should have already acquired the exclusive lock before calling this constructor\n  // The guard takes ownership of the lock\n  // Mark the frame as dirty since we're getting write access\n  frame_->is_dirty_ = true;\n}",
  "old_string": "WritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                               std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                               std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)) {\n  UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\");\n}",

  "newString": "WritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                               std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                               std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),\n      is_valid_(true) {\n  // The caller (BPM) should have already acquired the exclusive lock before calling this constructor\n  // The guard takes ownership of the lock\n  // Mark the frame as dirty since we're getting write access\n  frame_->is_dirty_ = true;\n}",

  "originalFile": "//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// page_guard.cpp\n//\n// Identification: src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#include \"storage/page/page_guard.h\"\n#include <memory>\n#include \"buffer/arc_replacer.h\"\n#include \"common/macros.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\n/**\n * @brief The only constructor for an RAII `ReadPageGuard` that creates a valid guard.\n *\n * Note that only the buffer pool manager is allowed to call this constructor.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param frame A shared pointer to the frame that holds the page we want to protect.\n * @param replacer A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's replacer.\n * @param bpm_latch A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's latch.\n * @param disk_scheduler A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's disk scheduler.\n */\nReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                             std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                             std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),\n      is_valid_(true) {\n  // The caller (BPM) should have already acquired the read lock before calling this constructor\n  // The guard takes ownership of the lock\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move constructor for `ReadPageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n */\nReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept\n    : page_id_(that.page_id_),\n      frame_(std::move(that.frame_)),\n      replacer_(std::move(that.replacer_)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(that.bpm_latch_)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(that.disk_scheduler_)),\n      is_valid_(that.is_valid_) {\n  // Invalidate the moved-from guard\n  that.is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move assignment operator for `ReadPageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each, and for the current object, make sure you release any resources it might be\n * holding on to.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n * @return ReadPageGuard& The newly valid `ReadPageGuard`.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::operator=(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept -> ReadPageGuard & {\n  if (this != &that) {\n    // First, drop any resources this guard might hold\n    Drop();\n\n    // Move the resources from 'that' to 'this'\n    page_id_ = that.page_id_;\n    frame_ = std::move(that.frame_);\n    replacer_ = std::move(that.replacer_);\n    bpm_latch_ = std::move(that.bpm_latch_);\n    disk_scheduler_ = std::move(that.disk_scheduler_);\n    is_valid_ = that.is_valid_;\n\n    // Invalidate the moved-from guard\n    that.is_valid_ = false;\n  }\n  return *this;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets the page ID of the page this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::GetPageId() const -> page_id_t {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return page_id_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a `const` pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::GetData() const -> const char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return frame_->GetData();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Returns whether the page is dirty (modified but not flushed to the disk).\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::IsDirty() const -> bool {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return frame_->is_dirty_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes this page's data safely to disk.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid ReadPageGuard::Flush() {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n\n  if (frame_->is_dirty_) {\n    // Write the page to disk\n    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n    DiskRequest request;\n    request.is_write_ = true;\n    request.data_ = frame_->GetDataMut();\n    request.page_id_ = page_id_;\n    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n    auto future = promise.get_future();\n    request.callback_ = std::move(promise);\n    requests.push_back(std::move(request));\n    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n    future.get();\n\n    frame_->is_dirty_ = false;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Manually drops a valid `ReadPageGuard`'s data. If this guard is invalid, this function does nothing.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * Make sure you don't double free! Also, think **very** **VERY** carefully about what resources you own and the order\n * in which you release those resources. If you get the ordering wrong, you will very likely fail one of the later\n * Gradescope tests. You may also want to take the buffer pool manager's latch in a very specific scenario...\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid ReadPageGuard::Drop() {\n  if (!is_valid_) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Unlock the shared lock on the frame\n  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock_shared();\n\n  // Decrement pin count and potentially set evictable\n  size_t old_pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1);\n  if (old_pin_count == 1) {\n    // This was the last pin, set the frame as evictable\n    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);\n    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);\n  }\n\n  // Invalidate the guard\n  is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/** @brief The destructor for `ReadPageGuard`. This destructor simply calls `Drop()`. */\nReadPageGuard::~ReadPageGuard() { Drop(); }\n\n/**********************************************************************************************************************/\n/**********************************************************************************************************************/\n/**********************************************************************************************************************/\n\n/**\n * @brief The only constructor for an RAII `WritePageGuard` that creates a valid guard.\n *\n * Note that only the buffer pool manager is allowed to call this constructor.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to write to.\n * @param frame A shared pointer to the frame that holds the page we want to protect.\n * @param replacer A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's replacer.\n * @param bpm_latch A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's latch.\n * @param disk_scheduler A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's disk scheduler.\n */\nWritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                               std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                               std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)) {\n  UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\");\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move constructor for `WritePageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n */\nWritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(WritePageGuard &&that) noexcept {}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move assignment operator for `WritePageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each, and for the current object, make sure you release any resources it might be\n * holding on to.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n * @return WritePageGuard& The newly valid `WritePageGuard`.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::operator=(WritePageGuard &&that) noexcept -> WritePageGuard & { return *this; }\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets the page ID of the page this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetPageId() const -> page_id_t {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return page_id_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a `const` pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetData() const -> const char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return frame_->GetData();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a mutable pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetDataMut() -> char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return frame_->GetDataMut();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Returns whether the page is dirty (modified but not flushed to the disk).\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::IsDirty() const -> bool {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return frame_->is_dirty_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes this page's data safely to disk.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid WritePageGuard::Flush() { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Manually drops a valid `WritePageGuard`'s data.   "filePath": "/workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp",
  "newString": "auto WritePageGuard::operator=(WritePageGuard &&that) noexcept -> WritePageGuard & {\n  if (this != &that) {\n    // First, drop any resources this guard might hold\n    Drop();\n\n    // Move the resources from 'that' to 'this'\n    page_id_ = that.page_id_;\n    frame_ = std::move(that.frame_);\n    replacer_ = std::move(that.replacer_);\n    bpm_latch_ = std::move(that.bpm_latch_);\n    disk_scheduler_ = std::move(that.disk_scheduler_);\n    is_valid_ = that.is_valid_;\n\n    // Invalidate the moved-from guard\n    that.is_valid_ = false;\n  }\n  return *this;\n}",

  "originalFile": "//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// page_guard.cpp\n//\n// Identification: src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#include \"storage/page/page_guard.h\"\n#include <memory>\n#include \"buffer/arc_replacer.h\"\n#include \"common/macros.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\n/**\n * @brief The only constructor for an RAII `ReadPageGuard` that creates a valid guard.\n *\n * Note that only the buffer pool manager is allowed to call this constructor.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param frame A shared pointer to the frame that holds the page we want to protect.\n * @param replacer A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's replacer.\n * @param bpm_latch A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's latch.\n * @param disk_scheduler A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's disk scheduler.\n */\nReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                             std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                             std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),\n      is_valid_(true) {\n  // The caller (BPM) should have already acquired the read lock before calling this constructor\n  // The guard takes ownership of the lock\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move constructor for `ReadPageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n */\nReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept\n    : page_id_(that.page_id_),\n      frame_(std::move(that.frame_)),\n      replacer_(std::move(that.replacer_)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(that.bpm_latch_)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(that.disk_scheduler_)),\n      is_valid_(that.is_valid_) {\n  // Invalidate the moved-from guard\n  that.is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move assignment operator for `ReadPageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each, and for the current object, make sure you release any resources it might be\n * holding on to.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n * @return ReadPageGuard& The newly valid `ReadPageGuard`.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::operator=(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept -> ReadPageGuard & {\n  if (this != &that) {\n    // First, drop any resources this guard might hold\n    Drop();\n\n    // Move the resources from 'that' to 'this'\n    page_id_ = that.page_id_;\n    frame_ = std::move(that.frame_);\n    replacer_ = std::move(that.replacer_);\n    bpm_latch_ = std::move(that.bpm_latch_);\n    disk_scheduler_ = std::move(that.disk_scheduler_);\n    is_valid_ = that.is_valid_;\n\n    // Invalidate the moved-from guard\n    that.is_valid_ = false;\n  }\n  return *this;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets the page ID of the page this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::GetPageId() const -> page_id_t {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return page_id_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a `const` pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::GetData() const -> const char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return frame_->GetData();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Returns whether the page is dirty (modified but not flushed to the disk).\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::IsDirty() const -> bool {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return frame_->is_dirty_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes this page's data safely to disk.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid ReadPageGuard::Flush() {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n\n  if (frame_->is_dirty_) {\n    // Write the page to disk\n    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n    DiskRequest request;\n    request.is_write_ = true;\n    request.data_ = frame_->GetDataMut();\n    request.page_id_ = page_id_;\n    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n    auto future = promise.get_future();\n    request.callback_ = std::move(promise);\n    requests.push_back(std::move(request));\n    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n    future.get();\n\n    frame_->is_dirty_ = false;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Manually drops a valid `ReadPageGuard`'s data. If this guard is invalid, this function does nothing.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * Make sure you don't double free! Also, think **very** **VERY** carefully about what resources you own and the order\n * in which you release those resources. If you get the ordering wrong, you will very likely fail one of the later\n * Gradescope tests. You may also want to take the buffer pool manager's latch in a very specific scenario...\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid ReadPageGuard::Drop() {\n  if (!is_valid_) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Unlock the shared lock on the frame\n  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock_shared();\n\n  // Decrement pin count and potentially set evictable\n  size_t old_pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1);\n  if (old_pin_count == 1) {\n    // This was the last pin, set the frame as evictable\n    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);\n    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);\n  }\n\n  // Invalidate the guard\n  is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/** @brief The destructor for `ReadPageGuard`. This destructor simply calls `Drop()`. */\nReadPageGuard::~ReadPageGuard() { Drop(); }\n\n/**********************************************************************************************************************/\n/**********************************************************************************************************************/\n/**********************************************************************************************************************/\n\n/**\n * @brief The only constructor for an RAII `WritePageGuard` that creates a valid guard.\n *\n * Note that only the buffer pool manager is allowed to call this constructor.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to write to.\n * @param frame A shared pointer to the frame that holds the page we want to protect.\n * @param replacer A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's replacer.\n * @param bpm_latch A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's latch.\n * @param disk_scheduler A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's disk scheduler.\n */\nWritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                               std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                               std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),\n      is_valid_(true) {\n  // The caller (BPM) should have already acquired the exclusive lock before calling this constructor\n  // The guard takes ownership of the lock\n  // Mark the frame as dirty since we're getting write access\n  frame_->is_dirty_ = true;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move constructor for `WritePageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n */\nWritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(WritePageGuard &&that) noexcept\n    : page_id_(that.page_id_),\n      frame_(std::move(that.frame_)),\n      replacer_(std::move(that.replacer_)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(that.bpm_latch_)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(that.disk_scheduler_)),\n      is_valid_(that.is_valid_) {\n  // Invalidate the moved-from guard\n  that.is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move assignment operator for `WritePageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each, and for the current object, make sure you release any resources it might be\n * holding on to.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n * @return WritePageGuard& The newly valid `WritePageGuard`.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::operator=(WritePageGuard &&that) noexcept -> WritePageGuard & { return *this; }\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets the page ID of the page this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetPageId() const -> page_id_t {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return page_id_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a `const` pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetData() const -> const char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return frame_->GetData();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a mutable pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetDataMut() -> char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return frame_->GetDataMut();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Returns whether the page is dirty (modified but not flushed to the disk).\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::IsDirty() const -> bool {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return frame_->is_dirty_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes this page's data safely to disk.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid WritePageGuard::Flush() { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Manually drops a valid `WritePageGuard`'s data.   request.callback_ = std::move(promise);\n    requests.push_back(std::move(request));\n    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n    future.get();\n\n    frame_->is_dirty_ = false;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Manually drops a valid `ReadPageGuard`'s data. If this guard is invalid, this function does nothing.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * Make sure you don't double free! Also, think **very** **VERY** carefully about what resources you own and the order\n * in which you release those resources. If you get the ordering wrong, you will very likely fail one of the later\n * Gradescope tests. You may also want to take the buffer pool manager's latch in a very specific scenario...\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid ReadPageGuard::Drop() {\n  if (!is_valid_) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Unlock the shared lock on the frame\n  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock_shared();\n\n  // Decrement pin count and potentially set evictable\n  size_t old_pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1);\n  if (old_pin_count == 1) {\n    // This was the last pin, set the frame as evictable\n    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);\n    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);\n  }\n\n  // Invalidate the guard\n  is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/** @brief The destructor for `ReadPageGuard`. This destructor simply calls `Drop()`. */\nReadPageGuard::~ReadPageGuard() { Drop(); }\n\n/**********************************************************************************************************************/\n/**********************************************************************************************************************/\n/**********************************************************************************************************************/\n\n/**\n * @brief The only constructor for an RAII `WritePageGuard` that creates a valid guard.\n *\n * Note that only the buffer pool manager is allowed to call this constructor.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to write to.\n * @param frame A shared pointer to the frame that holds the page we want to protect.\n * @param replacer A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's replacer.\n * @param bpm_latch A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's latch.\n * @param disk_scheduler A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's disk scheduler.\n */\nWritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                               std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                               std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),\n      is_valid_(true) {\n  // The caller (BPM) should have already acquired the exclusive lock before calling this constructor\n  // The guard takes ownership of the lock\n  // Mark the frame as dirty since we're getting write access\n  frame_->is_dirty_ = true;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move constructor for `WritePageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n */\nWritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(WritePageGuard &&that) noexcept\n    : page_id_(that.page_id_),\n      frame_(std::move(that.frame_)),\n      replacer_(std::move(that.replacer_)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(that.bpm_latch_)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(that.disk_scheduler_)),\n      is_valid_(that.is_valid_) {\n  // Invalidate the moved-from guard\n  that.is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move assignment operator for `WritePageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each, and for the current object, make sure you release any resources it might be\n * holding on to.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n * @return WritePageGuard& The newly valid `WritePageGuard`.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::operator=(WritePageGuard &&that) noexcept -> WritePageGuard & {\n  if (this != &that) {\n    // First, drop any resources this guard might hold\n    Drop();\n\n    // Move the resources from 'that' to 'this'\n    page_id_ = that.page_id_;\n    frame_ = std::move(that.frame_);\n    replacer_ = std::move(that.replacer_);\n    bpm_latch_ = std::move(that.bpm_latch_);\n    disk_scheduler_ = std::move(that.disk_scheduler_);\n    is_valid_ = that.is_valid_;\n\n    // Invalidate the moved-from guard\n    that.is_valid_ = false;\n  }\n  return *this;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets the page ID of the page this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetPageId() const -> page_id_t {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return page_id_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a `const` pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetData() const -> const char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return frame_->GetData();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a mutable pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetDataMut() -> char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to
