70. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp",
  "new_string": "auto BufferPoolManager::CheckedWritePage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<WritePageGuard> {\n  std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame;\n  frame_id_t frame_id;\n\n  {\n    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);\n\n    // Case 1: Page is already in the buffer pool\n    auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);\n    if (it != page_table_.end()) {\n      frame_id = it->second;\n      frame = frames_[frame_id];\n\n      // Increment pin count\n      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);\n\n      // Mark as not evictable\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n\n      // Record access for replacement policy\n      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n    } else {\n      // Case 2: Need to bring page into memory\n      // Try to find a free frame first\n      if (!free_frames_.empty()) {\n        frame_id = free_frames_.front();\n        free_frames_.pop_front();\n        frame = frames_[frame_id];\n      } else {\n        // Case 3: Need to evict a frame\n        auto evicted_frame_id = replacer_->Evict();\n        if (!evicted_frame_id.has_value()) {\n          // No frames can be evicted\n          return std::nullopt;\n        }\n        frame_id = evicted_frame_id.value();\n        frame = frames_[frame_id];\n\n        // Find the page that was in this frame and remove it from page_table_\n        for (auto pt_it = page_table_.begin(); pt_it != page_table_.end(); ++pt_it) {\n          if (pt_it->second == frame_id) {\n            // If the page is dirty, write it to disk first\n            if (frame->is_dirty_) {\n              std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n              DiskRequest req;\n              req.is_write_ = true;\n              req.data_ = frame->GetDataMut();\n              req.page_id_ = pt_it->first;\n              auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n              auto future = promise.get_future();\n              req.callback_ = std::move(promise);\n              requests.push_back(std::move(req));\n              disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n              future.get();\n            }\n            page_table_.erase(pt_it);\n            break;\n          }\n        }\n      }\n\n      // Reset the frame\n      frame->Reset();\n\n      // Read the page from disk\n      std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n      DiskRequest req;\n      req.is_write_ = false;\n      req.data_ = frame->GetDataMut();\n      req.page_id_ = page_id;\n      auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n      auto future = promise.get_future();\n      req.callback_ = std::move(promise);\n      requests.push_back(std::move(req));\n      disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n      future.get();\n\n      // Update the page table\n      page_table_[page_id] = frame_id;\n\n      // Increment pin count\n      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);\n\n      // Record access for replacement policy (this creates the entry in replacer)\n      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n\n      // Mark as not evictable\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Acquire the exclusive lock on the frame (outside the BPM latch to prevent deadlocks)\n  frame->rwlatch_.lock();\n\n  // Create and return the WritePageGuard\n  return WritePageGuard(page_id, frame, replacer_, bpm_latch_, disk_scheduler_);\n}",
  "old_string": "auto BufferPoolManager::CheckedWritePage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<WritePageGuard> {\n  UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\");\n}",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp",
  "newString": "auto BufferPoolManager::CheckedWritePage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<WritePageGuard> {\n  std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame;\n  frame_id_t frame_id;\n\n  {\n    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);\n\n    // Case 1: Page is already in the buffer pool\n    auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);\n    if (it != page_table_.end()) {\n      frame_id = it->second;\n      frame = frames_[frame_id];\n\n      // Increment pin count\n      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);\n\n      // Mark as not evictable\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n\n      // Record access for replacement policy\n      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n    } else {\n      // Case 2: Need to bring page into memory\n      // Try to find a free frame first\n      if (!free_frames_.empty()) {\n        frame_id = free_frames_.front();\n        free_frames_.pop_front();\n        frame = frames_[frame_id];\n      } else {\n        // Case 3: Need to evict a frame\n        auto evicted_frame_id = replacer_->Evict();\n        if (!evicted_frame_id.has_value()) {\n          // No frames can be evicted\n          return std::nullopt;\n        }\n        frame_id = evicted_frame_id.value();\n        frame = frames_[frame_id];\n\n        // Find the page that was in this frame and remove it from page_table_\n        for (auto pt_it = page_table_.begin(); pt_it != page_table_.end(); ++pt_it) {\n          if (pt_it->second == frame_id) {\n            // If the page is dirty, write it to disk first\n            if (frame->is_dirty_) {\n              std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n              DiskRequest req;\n              req.is_write_ = true;\n              req.data_ = frame->GetDataMut();\n              req.page_id_ = pt_it->first;\n              auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n              auto future = promise.get_future();\n              req.callback_ = std::move(promise);\n              requests.push_back(std::move(req));\n              disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n              future.get();\n            }\n            page_table_.erase(pt_it);\n            break;\n          }\n        }\n      }\n\n      // Reset the frame\n      frame->Reset();\n\n      // Read the page from disk\n      std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n      DiskRequest req;\n      req.is_write_ = false;\n      req.data_ = frame->GetDataMut();\n      req.page_id_ = page_id;\n      auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n      auto future = promise.get_future();\n      req.callback_ = std::move(promise);\n      requests.push_back(std::move(req));\n      disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n      future.get();\n\n      // Update the page table\n      page_table_[page_id] = frame_id;\n\n      // Increment pin count\n      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);\n\n      // Record access for replacement policy (this creates the entry in replacer)\n      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n\n      // Mark as not evictable\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Acquire the exclusive lock on the frame (outside the BPM latch to prevent deadlocks)\n  frame->rwlatch_.lock();\n\n  // Create and return the WritePageGuard\n  return WritePageGuard(page_id, frame, replacer_, bpm_latch_, disk_scheduler_);\n}",
  "oldString": "auto BufferPoolManager::CheckedWritePage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<WritePageGuard> {\n  UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\");\n}",
  "originalFile": "//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// buffer_pool_manager.cpp\n//\n// Identification: src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#include \"buffer/buffer_pool_manager.h\"\n#include \"buffer/arc_replacer.h\"\n#include \"common/config.h\"\n#include \"common/macros.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\n/**\n * @brief The constructor for a `FrameHeader` that initializes all fields to default values.\n *\n * See the documentation for `FrameHeader` in \"buffer/buffer_pool_manager.h\" for more information.\n *\n * @param frame_id The frame ID / index of the frame we are creating a header for.\n */\nFrameHeader::FrameHeader(frame_id_t frame_id) : frame_id_(frame_id), data_(BUSTUB_PAGE_SIZE, 0) { Reset(); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Get a raw const pointer to the frame's data.\n *\n * @return const char* A pointer to immutable data that the frame stores.\n */\nauto FrameHeader::GetData() const -> const char * { return data_.data(); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Get a raw mutable pointer to the frame's data.\n *\n * @return char* A pointer to mutable data that the frame stores.\n */\nauto FrameHeader::GetDataMut() -> char * { return data_.data(); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Resets a `FrameHeader`'s member fields.\n */\nvoid FrameHeader::Reset() {\n  std::fill(data_.begin(), data_.end(), 0);\n  pin_count_.store(0);\n  is_dirty_ = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Creates a new `BufferPoolManager` instance and initializes all fields.\n *\n * See the documentation for `BufferPoolManager` in \"buffer/buffer_pool_manager.h\" for more information.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * We have implemented the constructor for you in a way that makes sense with our reference solution. You are free to\n * change anything you would like here if it doesn't fit with you implementation.\n *\n * Be warned, though! If you stray too far away from our guidance, it will be much harder for us to help you. Our\n * recommendation would be to first implement the buffer pool manager using the stepping stones we have provided.\n *\n * Once you have a fully working solution (all Gradescope test cases pass), then you can try more interesting things!\n *\n * @param num_frames The size of the buffer pool.\n * @param disk_manager The disk manager.\n * @param log_manager The log manager. Please ignore this for P1.\n */\nBufferPoolManager::BufferPoolManager(size_t num_frames, DiskManager *disk_manager, LogManager *log_manager)\n    : num_frames_(num_frames),\n      next_page_id_(0),\n      bpm_latch_(std::make_shared<std::mutex>()),\n      replacer_(std::make_shared<ArcReplacer>(num_frames)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::make_shared<DiskScheduler>(disk_manager)),\n      log_manager_(log_manager) {\n  // Not strictly necessary...\n  std::scoped_lock latch(*bpm_latch_);\n\n  // Initialize the monotonically increasing counter at 0.\n  next_page_id_.store(0);\n\n  // Allocate all of the in-memory frames up front.\n  frames_.reserve(num_frames_);\n\n  // The page table should have exactly `num_frames_` slots, corresponding to exactly `num_frames_` frames.\n  page_table_.reserve(num_frames_);\n\n  // Initialize all of the frame headers, and fill the free frame list with all possible frame IDs (since all frames are\n  // initially free).\n  for (size_t i = 0; i < num_frames_; i++) {\n    frames_.push_back(std::make_shared<FrameHeader>(i));\n    free_frames_.push_back(static_cast<int>(i));\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Destroys the `BufferPoolManager`, freeing up all memory that the buffer pool was using.\n */\nBufferPoolManager::~BufferPoolManager() = default;\n\n/**\n * @brief Returns the number of frames that this buffer pool manages.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::Size() const -> size_t { return num_frames_; }\n\n/**\n * @brief Allocates a new page on disk.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You will maintain a thread-safe, monotonically increasing counter in the form of a `std::atomic<page_id_t>`.\n * See the documentation on [atomics](https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/atomic/atomic) for more information.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @return The page ID of the newly allocated page.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::NewPage() -> page_id_t {\n  // Atomically increment and return the previous value as the new page ID\n  return next_page_id_.fetch_add(1);\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Removes a page from the database, both on disk and in memory.\n *\n * If the page is pinned in the buffer pool, this function does nothing and returns `false`. Otherwise, this function\n * removes the page from both disk and memory (if it is still in the buffer pool), returning `true`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * Think about all of the places that a page or a page's metadata could be, and use that to guide you on implementing\n * this function. You will probably want to implement this function _after_ you have implemented `CheckedReadPage` and\n * `CheckedWritePage`.\n *\n * You should call `DeallocatePage` in the disk scheduler to make the space available for new pages.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to delete.\n * @return `false` if the page exists but could not be deleted, `true` if the page didn't exist or deletion succeeded.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::DeletePage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Acquires an optional write-locked guard over a page of data. The user can specify an `AccessType` if needed.\n *\n * If it is not possible to bring the page of data into memory, this function will return a `std::nullopt`.\n *\n * Page data can _only_ be accessed via page guards. Users of this `BufferPoolManager` are expected to acquire either a\n * `ReadPageGuard` or a `WritePageGuard` depending on the mode in which they would like to access the data, which\n * ensures that any access of data is thread-safe.\n *\n * There can only be 1 `WritePageGuard` reading/writing a page at a time. This allows data access to be both immutable\n * and mutable, meaning the thread that owns the `WritePageGuard` is allowed to manipulate the page's data however they\n * want. If a user wants to have multiple threads reading the page at the same time, they must acquire a `ReadPageGuard`\n * with `CheckedReadPage` instead.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * There are three main cases that you will have to implement. The first two are relatively simple: one is when there is\n * plenty of available memory, and the other is when we don't actually need to perform any additional I/O. Think about\n * what exactly these two cases entail.\n *\n * The third case is the trickiest, and it is when we do not have any _easily_ available memory at our disposal. The\n * buffer pool is tasked with finding memory that it can use to bring in a page of memory, using the replacement\n * algorithm you implemented previously to find candidate frames for eviction.\n *\n * Once the buffer pool has identified a frame for eviction, several I/O operations may be necessary to bring in the\n * page of data we want into the frame.\n *\n * There is likely going to be a lot of shared code with `CheckedReadPage`, so you may find creating helper functions\n * useful.\n *\n * These two functions are the crux of this project, so we won't give you more hints than this. Good luck!\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The ID of the page we want to write to.\n * @param access_type The type of page access.\n * @return std::optional<WritePageGuard> An optional latch guard where if there are no more free frames (out of memory)\n * returns `std::nullopt`; otherwise, returns a `WritePageGuard` ensuring exclusive and mutable access to a page's data.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::CheckedWritePage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<WritePageGuard> {\n  UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\");\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Acquires an optional read-locked guard over a page of data. The user can specify an `AccessType` if needed.\n *\n * If it is not possible to bring the page of data into memory, this function will return a `std::nullopt`.\n *\n * Page data can _only_ be accessed via page guards. Users of this `BufferPoolManager` are expected to acquire either a\n * `ReadPageGuard` or a `WritePageGuard` depending on the mode in which they would like to access the data, which\n * ensures that any access of data is thread-safe.\n *\n * There can be any number of `ReadPageGuard`s reading the same page of data at a time across different threads.\n * However, all data access must be immutable. If a user wants to mutate the page's data, they must acquire a\n * `WritePageGuard` with `CheckedWritePage` instead.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * See the implementation details of `CheckedWritePage`.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param access_type The type of page access.\n * @return std::optional<ReadPageGuard> An optional latch guard where if there are no more free frames (out of memory)\n * returns `std::nullopt`; otherwise, returns a `ReadPageGuard` ensuring shared and read-only access to a page's data.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::CheckedReadPage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<ReadPageGuard> {\n  UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\");\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief A wrapper around `CheckedWritePage` that unwraps the inner value if it exists.\n *\n * If `CheckedWritePage` returns a `std::nullopt`, **this function aborts the entire process.**\n *\n * This function should **only** be used for testing and ergonomic's sake. If it is at all possible that the buffer pool\n * manager might run out of memory, then use `CheckedPageWrite` to allow you to handle that case.\n *\n * See the documentation for `CheckedPageWrite` for more information about implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param access_type The type of page access.\n * @return WritePageGuard A page guard ensuring exclusive and mutable access to a page's data.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::WritePage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> WritePageGuard {\n  auto guard_opt = CheckedWritePage(page_id, access_type);\n\n  if (!guard_opt.has_value()) {\n    fmt::println(stderr, \"\\n`CheckedWritePage` failed to bring in page {}\\n\", page_id);\n    std::abort();\n  }\n\n  return std::move(guard_opt).value();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief A wrapper around `CheckedReadPage` that unwraps the inner value if it exists.\n *\n * If `CheckedReadPage` returns a `std::nullopt`, **this function aborts the entire process.**\n *\n * This function should **only** be used for testing and ergonomic's sake. If it is at all possible that the buffer pool\n * manager might run out of memory, then use `CheckedPageWrite` to allow you to handle that case.\n *\n * See the documentation for `CheckedPageRead` for more information about implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param access_type The type of page access.\n * @return ReadPageGuard A page guard ensuring shared and read-only access to a page's data.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::ReadPage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> ReadPageGuard {\n  auto guard_opt = CheckedReadPage(page_id, access_type);\n\n  if (!guard_opt.has_value()) {\n    fmt::println(stderr, \"\\n`CheckedReadPage` failed to bring in page {}\\n\", page_id);\n    std::abort();\n  }\n\n  return std::move(guard_opt).value();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes a page's data out to disk unsafely.\n *\n * This function will write out a page's data to disk if it has been modified. If the given page is not in memory, this\n * function will return `false`.\n *\n * You should not take a lock on the page in this function.\n * This means that you should carefully consider when to toggle the `is_dirty_` bit.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You should probably leave implementing this function until after you have completed `CheckedReadPage` and\n * `CheckedWritePage`, as it will likely be much easier to understand what to do.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page to be flushed.\n * @return `false` if the page could not be found in the page table; otherwise, `true`.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::FlushPageUnsafe(page_id_t page_id) -> bool { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes a page's data out to disk safely.\n *\n * This function will write out a page's data to disk if it has been modified. If the given page is not in memory, this\n * function will return `false`.\n *\n * You should take a lock on the page in this function to ensure that a consistent state is flushed to disk.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You should probably leave implementing this function until after you have completed `CheckedReadPage`,\n * `CheckedWritePage`, and `Flush` in the page guards, as it will likely be much easier to understand what to do.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page to be flushed.\n * @return `false` if the page could not be found in the page table; otherwise, `true`.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::FlushPage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes all page data that is in memory to disk unsafely.\n *\n * You should not take locks on the pages in this function.\n * This means that you should carefully consider when to toggle the `is_dirty_` bit.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You should probably leave implementing this function until after you have completed `CheckedReadPage`,\n * `CheckedWritePage`, and `FlushPage`, as it will likely be much easier to understand what to do.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n */\nvoid BufferPoolManager::FlushAllPagesUnsafe() { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes all page data that is in memory to disk safely.\n *\n * You should take locks on the pages in this function to ensure that a consistent state is flushed to disk.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You should probably leave implementing this function until after you have completed `CheckedReadPage`,\n * `CheckedWritePage`, and `FlushPage`, as it will likely be much easier to understand what to do.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n */\nvoid BufferPoolManager::FlushAllPages() { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Retrieves the pin count of a page. If the page does not exist in memory, return `std::nullopt`.\n *\n * This function is thread safe. Callers may invoke this function in a multi-threaded environment where multiple threads\n * access the same page.\n *\n * This function is intended for testing purposes. If this function is implemented incorrectly, it will definitely cause\n * problems with the test suite and autograder.\n *\n * # Implementation\n *\n * We will use this function to test if your buffer pool manager is managing pin counts correctly. Since the\n * `pin_count_` field in `FrameHeader` is an atomic type, you do not need to take the latch on the frame that holds the\n * page we want to look at. Instead, you can simply use an atomic `load` to safely load the value stored. You will still\n * need to take the buffer pool latch, however.\n *\n * Again, if you are unfamiliar with atomic types, see the official C++ docs\n * [here](https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/atomic/atomic).\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to get the pin count of.\n * @return std::optional<size_t> The pin count if the page exists; otherwise, `std::nullopt`.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::GetPinCount(page_id_t page_id) -> std::optional<size_t> {\n  UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\");\n}\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "  * returns `std::nullopt`; otherwise, returns a `WritePageGuard` ensuring exclusive and mutable access to a page's data.",
        "  */",
        " auto BufferPoolManager::CheckedWritePage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<WritePageGuard> {",
        "-  UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\");",
        "+  std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame;",
        "+  frame_id_t frame_id;",
        "+",
        "+  {",
        "+    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);",
        "+",
        "+    // Case 1: Page is already in the buffer pool",
        "+    auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);",
        "+    if (it != page_table_.end()) {",
        "+      frame_id = it->second;",
        "+      frame = frames_[frame_id];",
        "+",
        "+      // Increment pin count",
        "+      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);",
        "+",
        "+      // Mark as not evictable",
        "+      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);",
        "+",
        "+      // Record access for replacement policy",
        "+      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);",
        "+    } else {",
        "+      // Case 2: Need to bring page into memory",
        "+      // Try to find a free frame first",
        "+      if (!free_frames_.empty()) {",
        "+        frame_id = free_frames_.front();",
        "+        free_frames_.pop_front();",
        "+        frame = frames_[frame_id];",
        "+      } else {",
        "+        // Case 3: Need to evict a frame",
        "+        auto evicted_frame_id = replacer_->Evict();",
        "+        if (!evicted_frame_id.has_value()) {",
        "+          // No frames can be evicted",
        "+          return std::nullopt;",
        "+        }",
        "+        frame_id = evicted_frame_id.value();",
        "+        frame = frames_[frame_id];",
        "+",
        "+        // Find the page that was in this frame and remove it from page_table_",
        "+        for (auto pt_it = page_table_.begin(); pt_it != page_table_.end(); ++pt_it) {",
        "+          if (pt_it->second == frame_id) {",
        "+            // If the page is dirty, write it to disk first",
        "+            if (frame->is_dirty_) {",
        "+              std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;",
        "+              DiskRequest req;",
        "+              req.is_write_ = true;",
        "+              req.data_ = frame->GetDataMut();",
        "+              req.page_id_ = pt_it->first;",
        "+              auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();",
        "+              auto future = promise.get_future();",
        "+              req.callback_ = std::move(promise);",
        "+              requests.push_back(std::move(req));",
        "+              disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);",
        "+              future.get();",
        "+            }",
        "+            page_table_.erase(pt_it);",
        "+            break;",
        "+          }",
        "+        }",
        "+      }",
        "+",
        "+      // Reset the frame",
        "+      frame->Reset();",
        "+",
        "+      // Read the page from disk",
        "+      std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;",
        "+      DiskRequest req;",
        "+      req.is_write_ = false;",
        "+      req.data_ = frame->GetDataMut();",
        "+      req.page_id_ = page_id;",
        "+      auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();",
        "+      auto future = promise.get_future();",
        "+      req.callback_ = std::move(promise);",
        "+      requests.push_back(std::move(req));",
        "+      disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);",
        "+      future.get();",
        "+",
        "+      // Update the page table",
        "+      page_table_[page_id] = frame_id;",
        "+",
        "+      // Increment pin count",
        "+      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);",
        "+",
        "+      // Record access for replacement policy (this creates the entry in replacer)",
        "+      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);",
        "+",
        "+      // Mark as not evictable",
        "+      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);",
        "+    }",
        "+  }",
        "+",
        "+  // Acquire the exclusive lock on the frame (outside the BPM latch to prevent deadlocks)",
        "+  frame->rwlatch_.lock();",
        "+",
        "+  // Create and return the WritePageGuard",
        "+  return WritePageGuard(page_id, frame, replacer_, bpm_latch_, disk_scheduler_);",
        " }",
        " ",
        " /**"
      ],
      "newLines": 101,
      "newStart": 183,
      "oldLines": 7,
      "oldStart": 183
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}
71. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp",
  "new_string": "auto BufferPoolManager::CheckedReadPage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<ReadPageGuard> {\n  std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame;\n  frame_id_t frame_id;\n\n  {\n    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);\n\n    // Case 1: Page is already in the buffer pool\n    auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);\n    if (it != page_table_.end()) {\n      frame_id = it->second;\n      frame = frames_[frame_id];\n\n      // Increment pin count\n      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);\n\n      // Mark as not evictable\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n\n      // Record access for replacement policy\n      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n    } else {\n      // Case 2: Need to bring page into memory\n      // Try to find a free frame first\n      if (!free_frames_.empty()) {\n        frame_id = free_frames_.front();\n        free_frames_.pop_front();\n        frame = frames_[frame_id];\n      } else {\n        // Case 3: Need to evict a frame\n        auto evicted_frame_id = replacer_->Evict();\n        if (!evicted_frame_id.has_value()) {\n          // No frames can be evicted\n          return std::nullopt;\n        }\n        frame_id = evicted_frame_id.value();\n        frame = frames_[frame_id];\n\n        // Find the page that was in this frame and remove it from page_table_\n        for (auto pt_it = page_table_.begin(); pt_it != page_table_.end(); ++pt_it) {\n          if (pt_it->second == frame_id) {\n            // If the page is dirty, write it to disk first\n            if (frame->is_dirty_) {\n              std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n              DiskRequest req;\n              req.is_write_ = true;\n              req.data_ = frame->GetDataMut();\n              req.page_id_ = pt_it->first;\n              auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n              auto future = promise.get_future();\n              req.callback_ = std::move(promise);\n              requests.push_back(std::move(req));\n              disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n              future.get();\n            }\n            page_table_.erase(pt_it);\n            break;\n          }\n        }\n      }\n\n      // Reset the frame\n      frame->Reset();\n\n      // Read the page from disk\n      std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n      DiskRequest req;\n      req.is_write_ = false;\n      req.data_ = frame->GetDataMut();\n      req.page_id_ = page_id;\n      auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n      auto future = promise.get_future();\n      req.callback_ = std::move(promise);\n      requests.push_back(std::move(req));\n      disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n      future.get();\n\n      // Update the page table\n      page_table_[page_id] = frame_id;\n\n      // Increment pin count\n      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);\n\n      // Record access for replacement policy (this creates the entry in replacer)\n      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n\n      // Mark as not evictable\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Acquire the shared lock on the frame (outside the BPM latch to prevent deadlocks)\n  frame->rwlatch_.lock_shared();\n\n  // Create and return the ReadPageGuard\n  return ReadPageGuard(page_id, frame, replacer_, bpm_latch_, disk_scheduler_);\n}",
  "old_string": "auto BufferPoolManager::CheckedReadPage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<ReadPageGuard> {\n  UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\");\n}",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp",
  "newString": "auto BufferPoolManager::CheckedReadPage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<ReadPageGuard> {\n  std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame;\n  frame_id_t frame_id;\n\n  {\n    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);\n\n    // Case 1: Page is already in the buffer pool\n    auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);\n    if (it != page_table_.end()) {\n      frame_id = it->second;\n      frame = frames_[frame_id];\n\n      // Increment pin count\n      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);\n\n      // Mark as not evictable\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n\n      // Record access for replacement policy\n      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n    } else {\n      // Case 2: Need to bring page into memory\n      // Try to find a free frame first\n      if (!free_frames_.empty()) {\n        frame_id = free_frames_.front();\n        free_frames_.pop_front();\n        frame = frames_[frame_id];\n      } else {\n        // Case 3: Need to evict a frame\n        auto evicted_frame_id = replacer_->Evict();\n        if (!evicted_frame_id.has_value()) {\n          // No frames can be evicted\n          return std::nullopt;\n        }\n        frame_id = evicted_frame_id.value();\n        frame = frames_[frame_id];\n\n        // Find the page that was in this frame and remove it from page_table_\n        for (auto pt_it = page_table_.begin(); pt_it != page_table_.end(); ++pt_it) {\n          if (pt_it->second == frame_id) {\n            // If the page is dirty, write it to disk first\n            if (frame->is_dirty_) {\n              std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n              DiskRequest req;\n              req.is_write_ = true;\n              req.data_ = frame->GetDataMut();\n              req.page_id_ = pt_it->first;\n              auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n              auto future = promise.get_future();\n              req.callback_ = std::move(promise);\n              requests.push_back(std::move(req));\n              disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n              future.get();\n            }\n            page_table_.erase(pt_it);\n            break;\n          }\n        }\n      }\n\n      // Reset the frame\n      frame->Reset();\n\n      // Read the page from disk\n      std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n      DiskRequest req;\n      req.is_write_ = false;\n      req.data_ = frame->GetDataMut();\n      req.page_id_ = page_id;\n      auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n      auto future = promise.get_future();\n      req.callback_ = std::move(promise);\n      requests.push_back(std::move(req));\n      disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n      future.get();\n\n      // Update the page table\n      page_table_[page_id] = frame_id;\n\n      // Increment pin count\n      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);\n\n      // Record access for replacement policy (this creates the entry in replacer)\n      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n\n      // Mark as not evictable\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Acquire the shared lock on the frame (outside the BPM latch to prevent deadlocks)\n  frame->rwlatch_.lock_shared();\n\n  // Create and return the ReadPageGuard\n  return ReadPageGuard(page_id, frame, replacer_, bpm_latch_, disk_scheduler_);\n}",
  "oldString": "auto BufferPoolManager::CheckedReadPage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<ReadPageGuard> {\n  UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\");\n}",
  "originalFile": "//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// buffer_pool_manager.cpp\n//\n// Identification: src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#include \"buffer/buffer_pool_manager.h\"\n#include \"buffer/arc_replacer.h\"\n#include \"common/config.h\"\n#include \"common/macros.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\n/**\n * @brief The constructor for a `FrameHeader` that initializes all fields to default values.\n *\n * See the documentation for `FrameHeader` in \"buffer/buffer_pool_manager.h\" for more information.\n *\n * @param frame_id The frame ID / index of the frame we are creating a header for.\n */\nFrameHeader::FrameHeader(frame_id_t frame_id) : frame_id_(frame_id), data_(BUSTUB_PAGE_SIZE, 0) { Reset(); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Get a raw const pointer to the frame's data.\n *\n * @return const char* A pointer to immutable data that the frame stores.\n */\nauto FrameHeader::GetData() const -> const char * { return data_.data(); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Get a raw mutable pointer to the frame's data.\n *\n * @return char* A pointer to mutable data that the frame stores.\n */\nauto FrameHeader::GetDataMut() -> char * { return data_.data(); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Resets a `FrameHeader`'s member fields.\n */\nvoid FrameHeader::Reset() {\n  std::fill(data_.begin(), data_.end(), 0);\n  pin_count_.store(0);\n  is_dirty_ = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Creates a new `BufferPoolManager` instance and initializes all fields.\n *\n * See the documentation for `BufferPoolManager` in \"buffer/buffer_pool_manager.h\" for more information.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * We have implemented the constructor for you in a way that makes sense with our reference solution. You are free to\n * change anything you would like here if it doesn't fit with you implementation.\n *\n * Be warned, though! If you stray too far away from our guidance, it will be much harder for us to help you. Our\n * recommendation would be to first implement the buffer pool manager using the stepping stones we have provided.\n *\n * Once you have a fully working solution (all Gradescope test cases pass), then you can try more interesting things!\n *\n * @param num_frames The size of the buffer pool.\n * @param disk_manager The disk manager.\n * @param log_manager The log manager. Please ignore this for P1.\n */\nBufferPoolManager::BufferPoolManager(size_t num_frames, DiskManager *disk_manager, LogManager *log_manager)\n    : num_frames_(num_frames),\n      next_page_id_(0),\n      bpm_latch_(std::make_shared<std::mutex>()),\n      replacer_(std::make_shared<ArcReplacer>(num_frames)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::make_shared<DiskScheduler>(disk_manager)),\n      log_manager_(log_manager) {\n  // Not strictly necessary...\n  std::scoped_lock latch(*bpm_latch_);\n\n  // Initialize the monotonically increasing counter at 0.\n  next_page_id_.store(0);\n\n  // Allocate all of the in-memory frames up front.\n  frames_.reserve(num_frames_);\n\n  // The page table should have exactly `num_frames_` slots, corresponding to exactly `num_frames_` frames.\n  page_table_.reserve(num_frames_);\n\n  // Initialize all of the frame headers, and fill the free frame list with all possible frame IDs (since all frames are\n  // initially free).\n  for (size_t i = 0; i < num_frames_; i++) {\n    frames_.push_back(std::make_shared<FrameHeader>(i));\n    free_frames_.push_back(static_cast<int>(i));\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Destroys the `BufferPoolManager`, freeing up all memory that the buffer pool was using.\n */\nBufferPoolManager::~BufferPoolManager() = default;\n\n/**\n * @brief Returns the number of frames that this buffer pool manages.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::Size() const -> size_t { return num_frames_; }\n\n/**\n * @brief Allocates a new page on disk.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You will maintain a thread-safe, monotonically increasing counter in the form of a `std::atomic<page_id_t>`.\n * See the documentation on [atomics](https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/atomic/atomic) for more information.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @return The page ID of the newly allocated page.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::NewPage() -> page_id_t {\n  // Atomically increment and return the previous value as the new page ID\n  return next_page_id_.fetch_add(1);\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Removes a page from the database, both on disk and in memory.\n *\n * If the page is pinned in the buffer pool, this function does nothing and returns `false`. Otherwise, this function\n * removes the page from both disk and memory (if it is still in the buffer pool), returning `true`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * Think about all of the places that a page or a page's metadata could be, and use that to guide you on implementing\n * this function. You will probably want to implement this function _after_ you have implemented `CheckedReadPage` and\n * `CheckedWritePage`.\n *\n * You should call `DeallocatePage` in the disk scheduler to make the space available for new pages.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to delete.\n * @return `false` if the page exists but could not be deleted, `true` if the page didn't exist or deletion succeeded.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::DeletePage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Acquires an optional write-locked guard over a page of data. The user can specify an `AccessType` if needed.\n *\n * If it is not possible to bring the page of data into memory, this function will return a `std::nullopt`.\n *\n * Page data can _only_ be accessed via page guards. Users of this `BufferPoolManager` are expected to acquire either a\n * `ReadPageGuard` or a `WritePageGuard` depending on the mode in which they would like to access the data, which\n * ensures that any access of data is thread-safe.\n *\n * There can only be 1 `WritePageGuard` reading/writing a page at a time. This allows data access to be both immutable\n * and mutable, meaning the thread that owns the `WritePageGuard` is allowed to manipulate the page's data however they\n * want. If a user wants to have multiple threads reading the page at the same time, they must acquire a `ReadPageGuard`\n * with `CheckedReadPage` instead.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * There are three main cases that you will have to implement. The first two are relatively simple: one is when there is\n * plenty of available memory, and the other is when we don't actually need to perform any additional I/O. Think about\n * what exactly these two cases entail.\n *\n * The third case is the trickiest, and it is when we do not have any _easily_ available memory at our disposal. The\n * buffer pool is tasked with finding memory that it can use to bring in a page of memory, using the replacement\n * algorithm you implemented previously to find candidate frames for eviction.\n *\n * Once the buffer pool has identified a frame for eviction, several I/O operations may be necessary to bring in the\n * page of data we want into the frame.\n *\n * There is likely going to be a lot of shared code with `CheckedReadPage`, so you may find creating helper functions\n * useful.\n *\n * These two functions are the crux of this project, so we won't give you more hints than this. Good luck!\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The ID of the page we want to write to.\n * @param access_type The type of page access.\n * @return std::optional<WritePageGuard> An optional latch guard where if there are no more free frames (out of memory)\n * returns `std::nullopt`; otherwise, returns a `WritePageGuard` ensuring exclusive and mutable access to a page's data.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::CheckedWritePage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<WritePageGuard> {\n  std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame;\n  frame_id_t frame_id;\n\n  {\n    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);\n\n    // Case 1: Page is already in the buffer pool\n    auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);\n    if (it != page_table_.end()) {\n      frame_id = it->second;\n      frame = frames_[frame_id];\n\n      // Increment pin count\n      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);\n\n      // Mark as not evictable\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n\n      // Record access for replacement policy\n      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n    } else {\n      // Case 2: Need to bring page into memory\n      // Try to find a free frame first\n      if (!free_frames_.empty()) {\n        frame_id = free_frames_.front();\n        free_frames_.pop_front();\n        frame = frames_[frame_id];\n      } else {\n        // Case 3: Need to evict a frame\n        auto evicted_frame_id = replacer_->Evict();\n        if (!evicted_frame_id.has_value()) {\n          // No frames can be evicted\n          return std::nullopt;\n        }\n        frame_id = evicted_frame_id.value();\n        frame = frames_[frame_id];\n\n        // Find the page that was in this frame and remove it from page_table_\n        for (auto pt_it = page_table_.begin(); pt_it != page_table_.end(); ++pt_it) {\n          if (pt_it->second == frame_id) {\n            // If the page is dirty, write it to disk first\n            if (frame->is_dirty_) {\n              std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n              DiskRequest req;\n              req.is_write_ = true;\n              req.data_ = frame->GetDataMut();\n              req.page_id_ = pt_it->first;\n              auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n              auto future = promise.get_future();\n              req.callback_ = std::move(promise);\n              requests.push_back(std::move(req));\n              disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n              future.get();\n            }\n            page_table_.erase(pt_it);\n            break;\n          }\n        }\n      }\n\n      // Reset the frame\n      frame->Reset();\n\n      // Read the page from disk\n      std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n      DiskRequest req;\n      req.is_write_ = false;\n      req.data_ = frame->GetDataMut();\n      req.page_id_ = page_id;\n      auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n      auto future = promise.get_future();\n      req.callback_ = std::move(promise);\n      requests.push_back(std::move(req));\n      disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n      future.get();\n\n      // Update the page table\n      page_table_[page_id] = frame_id;\n\n      // Increment pin count\n      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);\n\n      // Record access for replacement policy (this creates the entry in replacer)\n      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n\n      // Mark as not evictable\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Acquire the exclusive lock on the frame (outside the BPM latch to prevent deadlocks)\n  frame->rwlatch_.lock();\n\n  // Create and return the WritePageGuard\n  return WritePageGuard(page_id, frame, replacer_, bpm_latch_, disk_scheduler_);\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Acquires an optional read-locked guard over a page of data. The user can specify an `AccessType` if needed.\n *\n * If it is not possible to bring the page of data into memory, this function will return a `std::nullopt`.\n *\n * Page data can _only_ be accessed via page guards. Users of this `BufferPoolManager` are expected to acquire either a\n * `ReadPageGuard` or a `WritePageGuard` depending on the mode in which they would like to access the data, which\n * ensures that any access of data is thread-safe.\n *\n * There can be any number of `ReadPageGuard`s reading the same page of data at a time across different threads.\n * However, all data access must be immutable. If a user wants to mutate the page's data, they must acquire a\n * `WritePageGuard` with `CheckedWritePage` instead.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * See the implementation details of `CheckedWritePage`.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param access_type The type of page access.\n * @return std::optional<ReadPageGuard> An optional latch guard where if there are no more free frames (out of memory)\n * returns `std::nullopt`; otherwise, returns a `ReadPageGuard` ensuring shared and read-only access to a page's data.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::CheckedReadPage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<ReadPageGuard> {\n  UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\");\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief A wrapper around `CheckedWritePage` that unwraps the inner value if it exists.\n *\n * If `CheckedWritePage` returns a `std::nullopt`, **this function aborts the entire process.**\n *\n * This function should **only** be used for testing and ergonomic's sake. If it is at all possible that the buffer pool\n * manager might run out of memory, then use `CheckedPageWrite` to allow you to handle that case.\n *\n * See the documentation for `CheckedPageWrite` for more information about implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param access_type The type of page access.\n * @return WritePageGuard A page guard ensuring exclusive and mutable access to a page's data.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::WritePage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> WritePageGuard {\n  auto guard_opt = CheckedWritePage(page_id, access_type);\n\n  if (!guard_opt.has_value()) {\n    fmt::println(stderr, \"\\n`CheckedWritePage` failed to bring in page {}\\n\", page_id);\n    std::abort();\n  }\n\n  return std::move(guard_opt).value();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief A wrapper around `CheckedReadPage` that unwraps the inner value if it exists.\n *\n * If `CheckedReadPage` returns a `std::nullopt`, **this function aborts the entire process.**\n *\n * This function should **only** be used for testing and ergonomic's sake. If it is at all possible that the buffer pool\n * manager might run out of memory, then use `CheckedPageWrite` to allow you to handle that case.\n *\n * See the documentation for `CheckedPageRead` for more information about implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param access_type The type of page access.\n * @return ReadPageGuard A page guard ensuring shared and read-only access to a page's data.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::ReadPage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> ReadPageGuard {\n  auto guard_opt = CheckedReadPage(page_id, access_type);\n\n  if (!guard_opt.has_value()) {\n    fmt::println(stderr, \"\\n`CheckedReadPage` failed to bring in page {}\\n\", page_id);\n    std::abort();\n  }\n\n  return std::move(guard_opt).value();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes a page's data out to disk unsafely.\n *\n * This function will write out a page's data to disk if it has been modified. If the given page is not in memory, this\n * function will return `false`.\n *\n * You should not take a lock on the page in this function.\n * This means that you should carefully consider when to toggle the `is_dirty_` bit.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You should probably leave implementing this function until after you have completed `CheckedReadPage` and\n * `CheckedWritePage`, as it will likely be much easier to understand what to do.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page to be flushed.\n * @return `false` if the page could not be found in the page table; otherwise, `true`.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::FlushPageUnsafe(page_id_t page_id) -> bool { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes a page's data out to disk safely.\n *\n * This function will write out a page's data to disk if it has been modified. If the given page is not in memory, this\n * function will return `false`.\n *\n * You should take a lock on the page in this function to ensure that a consistent state is flushed to disk.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You should probably leave implementing this function until after you have completed `CheckedReadPage`,\n * `CheckedWritePage`, and `Flush` in the page guards, as it will likely be much easier to understand what to do.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page to be flushed.\n * @return `false` if the page could not be found in the page table; otherwise, `true`.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::FlushPage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes all page data that is in memory to disk unsafely.\n *\n * You should not take locks on the pages in this function.\n * This means that you should carefully consider when to toggle the `is_dirty_` bit.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You should probably leave implementing this function until after you have completed `CheckedReadPage`,\n * `CheckedWritePage`, and `FlushPage`, as it will likely be much easier to understand what to do.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n */\nvoid BufferPoolManager::FlushAllPagesUnsafe() { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes all page data that is in memory to disk safely.\n *\n * You should take locks on the pages in this function to ensure that a consistent state is flushed to disk.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You should probably leave implementing this function until after you have completed `CheckedReadPage`,\n * `CheckedWritePage`, and `FlushPage`, as it will likely be much easier to understand what to do.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n */\nvoid BufferPoolManager::FlushAllPages() { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Retrieves the pin count of a page. If the page does not exist in memory, return `std::nullopt`.\n *\n * This function is thread safe. Callers may invoke this function in a multi-threaded environment where multiple threads\n * access the same page.\n *\n * This function is intended for testing purposes. If this function is implemented incorrectly, it will definitely cause\n * problems with the test suite and autograder.\n *\n * # Implementation\n *\n * We will use this function to test if your buffer pool manager is managing pin counts correctly. Since the\n * `pin_count_` field in `FrameHeader` is an atomic type, you do not need to take the latch on the frame that holds the\n * page we want to look at. Instead, you can simply use an atomic `load` to safely load the value stored. You will still\n * need to take the buffer pool latch, however.\n *\n * Again, if you are unfamiliar with atomic types, see the official C++ docs\n * [here](https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/atomic/atomic).\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to get the pin count of.\n * @return std::optional<size_t> The pin count if the page exists; otherwise, `std::nullopt`.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::GetPinCount(page_id_t page_id) -> std::optional<size_t> {\n  UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\");\n}\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n",

72. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp",
  "new_string": "auto BufferPoolManager::DeletePage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool {\n  std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);\n\n  // Check if page is in the buffer pool\n  auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);\n  if (it == page_table_.end()) {\n    // Page is not in memory - deallocate from disk\n    disk_scheduler_->DeallocatePage(page_id);\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  frame_id_t frame_id = it->second;\n  auto frame = frames_[frame_id];\n\n  // Check if the page is pinned\n  if (frame->pin_count_.load() > 0) {\n    // Cannot delete a pinned page\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  // Remove from page table\n  page_table_.erase(it);\n\n  // Remove from replacer\n  replacer_->Remove(frame_id);\n\n  // Reset the frame\n  frame->Reset();\n\n  // Add frame back to free list\n  free_frames_.push_back(frame_id);\n\n  // Deallocate the page from disk\n  disk_scheduler_->DeallocatePage(page_id);\n\n  return true;\n}",
  "old_string": "auto BufferPoolManager::DeletePage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp",
  "newString": "auto BufferPoolManager::DeletePage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool {\n  std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);\n\n  // Check if page is in the buffer pool\n  auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);\n  if (it == page_table_.end()) {\n    // Page is not in memory - deallocate from disk\n    disk_scheduler_->DeallocatePage(page_id);\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  frame_id_t frame_id = it->second;\n  auto frame = frames_[frame_id];\n\n  // Check if the page is pinned\n  if (frame->pin_count_.load() > 0) {\n    // Cannot delete a pinned page\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  // Remove from page table\n  page_table_.erase(it);\n\n  // Remove from replacer\n  replacer_->Remove(frame_id);\n\n  // Reset the frame\n  frame->Reset();\n\n  // Add frame back to free list\n  free_frames_.push_back(frame_id);\n\n  // Deallocate the page from disk\n  disk_scheduler_->DeallocatePage(page_id);\n\n  return true;\n}",

  "originalFile": "//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// buffer_pool_manager.cpp\n//\n// Identification: src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#include \"buffer/buffer_pool_manager.h\"\n#include \"buffer/arc_replacer.h\"\n#include \"common/config.h\"\n#include \"common/macros.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\n/**\n * @brief The constructor for a `FrameHeader` that initializes all fields to default values.\n *\n * See the documentation for `FrameHeader` in \"buffer/buffer_pool_manager.h\" for more information.\n *\n * @param frame_id The frame ID / index of the frame we are creating a header for.\n */\nFrameHeader::FrameHeader(frame_id_t frame_id) : frame_id_(frame_id), data_(BUSTUB_PAGE_SIZE, 0) { Reset(); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Get a raw const pointer to the frame's data.\n *\n * @return const char* A pointer to immutable data that the frame stores.\n */\nauto FrameHeader::GetData() const -> const char * { return data_.data(); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Get a raw mutable pointer to the frame's data.\n *\n * @return char* A pointer to mutable data that the frame stores.\n */\nauto FrameHeader::GetDataMut() -> char * { return data_.data(); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Resets a `FrameHeader`'s member fields.\n */\nvoid FrameHeader::Reset() {\n  std::fill(data_.begin(), data_.end(), 0);\n  pin_count_.store(0);\n  is_dirty_ = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Creates a new `BufferPoolManager` instance and initializes all fields.\n *\n * See the documentation for `BufferPoolManager` in \"buffer/buffer_pool_manager.h\" for more information.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * We have implemented the constructor for you in a way that makes sense with our reference solution. You are free to\n * change anything you would like here if it doesn't fit with you implementation.\n *\n * Be warned, though! If you stray too far away from our guidance, it will be much harder for us to help you. Our\n * recommendation would be to first implement the buffer pool manager using the stepping stones we have provided.\n *\n * Once you have a fully working solution (all Gradescope test cases pass), then you can try more interesting things!\n *\n * @param num_frames The size of the buffer pool.\n * @param disk_manager The disk manager.\n * @param log_manager The log manager. Please ignore this for P1.\n */\nBufferPoolManager::BufferPoolManager(size_t num_frames, DiskManager *disk_manager, LogManager *log_manager)\n    : num_frames_(num_frames),\n      next_page_id_(0),\n      bpm_latch_(std::make_shared<std::mutex>()),\n      replacer_(std::make_shared<ArcReplacer>(num_frames)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::make_shared<DiskScheduler>(disk_manager)),\n      log_manager_(log_manager) {\n  // Not strictly necessary...\n  std::scoped_lock latch(*bpm_latch_);\n\n  // Initialize the monotonically increasing counter at 0.\n  next_page_id_.store(0);\n\n  // Allocate all of the in-memory frames up front.\n  frames_.reserve(num_frames_);\n\n  // The page table should have exactly `num_frames_` slots, corresponding to exactly `num_frames_` frames.\n  page_table_.reserve(num_frames_);\n\n  // Initialize all of the frame headers, and fill the free frame list with all possible frame IDs (since all frames are\n  // initially free).\n  for (size_t i = 0; i < num_frames_; i++) {\n    frames_.push_back(std::make_shared<FrameHeader>(i));\n    free_frames_.push_back(static_cast<int>(i));\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Destroys the `BufferPoolManager`, freeing up all memory that the buffer pool was using.\n */\nBufferPoolManager::~BufferPoolManager() = default;\n\n/**\n * @brief Returns the number of frames that this buffer pool manages.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::Size() const -> size_t { return num_frames_; }\n\n/**\n * @brief Allocates a new page on disk.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You will maintain a thread-safe, monotonically increasing counter in the form of a `std::atomic<page_id_t>`.\n * See the documentation on [atomics](https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/atomic/atomic) for more information.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @return The page ID of the newly allocated page.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::NewPage() -> page_id_t {\n  // Atomically increment and return the previous value as the new page ID\n  return next_page_id_.fetch_add(1);\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Removes a page from the database, both on disk and in memory.\n *\n * If the page is pinned in the buffer pool, this function does nothing and returns `false`. Otherwise, this function\n * removes the page from both disk and memory (if it is still in the buffer pool), returning `true`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * Think about all of the places that a page or a page's metadata could be, and use that to guide you on implementing\n * this function. You will probably want to implement this function _after_ you have implemented `CheckedReadPage` and\n * `CheckedWritePage`.\n *\n * You should call `DeallocatePage` in the disk scheduler to make the space available for new pages.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to delete.\n * @return `false` if the page exists but could not be deleted, `true` if the page didn't exist or deletion succeeded.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::DeletePage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Acquires an optional write-locked guard over a page of data. The user can specify an `AccessType` if needed.\n *\n * If it is not possible to bring the page of data into memory, this function will return a `std::nullopt`.\n *\n * Page data can _only_ be accessed via page guards. Users of this `BufferPoolManager` are expected to acquire either a\n * `ReadPageGuard` or a `WritePageGuard` depending on the mode in which they would like to access the data, which\n * ensures that any access of data is thread-safe.\n *\n * There can only be 1 `WritePageGuard` reading/writing a page at a time. This allows data access to be both immutable\n * and mutable, meaning the thread that owns the `WritePageGuard` is allowed to manipulate the page's data however they\n * want. If a user wants to have multiple threads reading the page at the same time, they must acquire a `ReadPageGuard`\n * with `CheckedReadPage` instead.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * There are three main cases that you will have to implement. The first two are relatively simple: one is when there is\n * plenty of available memory, and the other is when we don't actually need to perform any additional I/O. Think about\n * what exactly these two cases entail.\n *\n * The third case is the trickiest, and it is when we do not have any _easily_ available memory at our disposal. The\n * buffer pool is tasked with finding memory that it can use to bring in a page of memory, using the replacement\n * algorithm you implemented previously to find candidate frames for eviction.\n *\n * Once the buffer pool has identified a frame for eviction, several I/O operations may be necessary to bring in the\n * page of data we want into the frame.\n *\n * There is likely going to be a lot of shared code with `CheckedReadPage`, so you may find creating helper functions\n * useful.\n *\n * These two functions are the crux of this project, so we won't give you more hints than this. Good luck!\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The ID of the page we want to write to.\n * @param access_type The type of page access.\n * @return std::optional<WritePageGuard> An optional latch guard where if there are no more free frames (out of memory)\n * returns `std::nullopt`; otherwise, returns a `WritePageGuard` ensuring exclusive and mutable access to a page's data.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::CheckedWritePage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<WritePageGuard> {\n  std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame;\n  frame_id_t frame_id;\n\n  {\n    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);\n\n    // Case 1: Page is already in the buffer pool\n    auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);\n    if (it != page_table_.end()) {\n      frame_id = it->second;\n      frame = frames_[frame_id];\n\n      // Increment pin count\n      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);\n\n      // Mark as not evictable\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n\n      // Record access for replacement policy\n      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n    } else {\n      // Case 2: Need to bring page into memory\n      // Try to find a free frame first\n      if (!free_frames_.empty()) {\n        frame_id = free_frames_.front();\n        free_frames_.pop_front();\n        frame = frames_[frame_id];\n      } else {\n        // Case 3: Need to evict a frame\n        auto evicted_frame_id = replacer_->Evict();\n        if (!evicted_frame_id.has_value()) {\n          // No frames can be evicted\n          return std::nullopt;\n        }\n        frame_id = evicted_frame_id.value();\n        frame = frames_[frame_id];\n\n        // Find the page that was in this frame and remove it from page_table_\n        for (auto pt_it = page_table_.begin(); pt_it != page_table_.end(); ++pt_it) {\n          if (pt_it->second == frame_id) {\n            // If the page is dirty, write it to disk first\n            if (frame->is_dirty_) {\n              std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n              DiskRequest req;\n              req.is_write_ = true;\n              req.data_ = frame->GetDataMut();\n              req.page_id_ = pt_it->first;\n              auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n              auto future = promise.get_future();\n              req.callback_ = std::move(promise);\n              requests.push_back(std::move(req));\n              disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n              future.get();\n            }\n            page_table_.erase(pt_it);\n            break;\n          }\n        }\n      }\n\n      // Reset the frame\n      frame->Reset();\n\n      // Read the page from disk\n      std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n      DiskRequest req;\n      req.is_write_ = false;\n      req.data_ = frame->GetDataMut();\n      req.page_id_ = page_id;\n      auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n      auto future = promise.get_future();\n      req.callback_ = std::move(promise);\n      requests.push_back(std::move(req));\n      disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n      future.get();\n\n      // Update the page table\n      page_table_[page_id] = frame_id;\n\n      // Increment pin count\n      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);\n\n      // Record access for replacement policy (this creates the entry in replacer)\n      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n\n      // Mark as not evictable\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Acquire the exclusive lock on the frame (outside the BPM latch to prevent deadlocks)\n  frame->rwlatch_.lock();\n\n  // Create and return the WritePageGuard\n  return WritePageGuard(page_id, frame, replacer_, bpm_latch_, disk_scheduler_);\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Acquires an optional read-locked guard over a page of data. The user can specify an `AccessType` if needed.\n *\n * If it is not possible to bring the page of data into memory, this function will return a `std::nullopt`.\n *\n * Page data can _only_ be accessed via page guards. Users of this `BufferPoolManager` are expected to acquire either a\n * `ReadPageGuard` or a `WritePageGuard` depending on the mode in which they would like to access the data, which\n * ensures that any access of data is thread-safe.\n *\n * There can be any number of `ReadPageGuard`s reading the same page of data at a time across different threads.\n * However, all data access must be immutable. If a user wants to mutate the page's data, they must acquire a\n * `WritePageGuard` with `CheckedWritePage` instead.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * See the implementation details of `CheckedWritePage`.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param access_type The type of page access.\n * @return std::optional<ReadPageGuard> An optional latch guard where if there are no more free frames (out of memory)\n * returns `std::nullopt`; otherwise, returns a `ReadPageGuard` ensuring shared and read-only access to a page's data.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::CheckedReadPage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<ReadPageGuard> {\n  std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame;\n  frame_id_t frame_id;\n\n  {\n    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);\n\n    // Case 1: Page is already in the buffer pool\n    auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);\n    if (it != page_table_.end()) {\n      frame_id = it->second;\n      frame = frames_[frame_id];\n\n      // Increment pin count\n      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);\n\n      // Mark as not evictable\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n\n      // Record access for replacement policy\n      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n    } else {\n      // Case 2: Need to bring page into memory\n      // Try to find a free frame first\n      if (!free_frames_.empty()) {\n        frame_id = free_frames_.front();\n        free_frames_.pop_front();\n        frame = frames_[frame_id];\n      } else {\n        // Case 3: Need to evict a frame\n        auto evicted_frame_id = replacer_->Evict();\n        if (!evicted_frame_id.has_value()) {\n          // No frames can be evicted\n          return std::nullopt;\n        }\n        frame_id = evicted_frame_id.value();\n        frame = frames_[frame_id];\n\n        // Find the page that was in this frame and remove it from page_table_\n        for (auto pt_it = page_table_.begin(); pt_it != page_table_.end(); ++pt_it) {\n          if (pt_it->second == frame_id) {\n            // If the page is dirty, write it to disk first\n            if (frame->is_dirty_) {\n              std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n              DiskRequest req;\n              req.is_write_ = true;\n              req.data_ = frame->GetDataMut();\n              req.page_id_ = pt_it->first;\n              auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n              auto future = promise.get_future();\n              req.callback_ = std::move(promise);\n              requests.push_back(std::move(req));\n              disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n              future.get();\n            }\n            page_table_.erase(pt_it);\n            break;\n          }\n        }\n      }\n\n      // Reset the frame\n      frame->Reset();\n\n      // Read the page from disk\n      std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n      DiskRequest req;\n      req.is_write_ = false;\n      req.data_ = frame->GetDataMut();\n      req.page_id_ = page_id;\n      auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n      auto future = promise.get_future();\n      req.callback_ = std::move(promise);\n      requests.push_back(std::move(req));\n      disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n      future.get();\n\n      // Update the page table\n      page_table_[page_id] = frame_id;\n\n      // Increment pin count\n      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);\n\n      // Record access for replacement policy (this creates the entry in replacer)\n      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n\n      // Mark as not evictable\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Acquire the shared lock on the frame (outside the BPM latch to prevent deadlocks)\n  frame->rwlatch_.lock_shared();\n\n  // Create and return the ReadPageGuard\n  return ReadPageGuard(page_id, frame, replacer_, bpm_latch_, disk_scheduler_);\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief A wrapper around `CheckedWritePage` that unwraps the inner value if it exists.\n *\n * If `CheckedWritePage` returns a `std::nullopt`, **this function aborts the entire process.**\n *\n * This function should **only** be used for testing and ergonomic's sake. If it is at all possible that the buffer pool\n * manager might run out of memory, then use `CheckedPageWrite` to allow you to handle that case.\n *\n * See the documentation for `CheckedPageWrite` for more information about implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param access_type The type of page access.\n * @return WritePageGuard A page guard ensuring exclusive and mutable access to a page's data.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::WritePage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> WritePageGuard {\n  auto guard_opt = CheckedWritePage(page_id, access_type);\n\n  if (!guard_opt.has_value()) {\n    fmt::println(stderr, \"\\n`CheckedWritePage` failed to bring in page {}\\n\", page_id);\n    std::abort();\n  }\n\n  return std::move(guard_opt).value();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief A wrapper around `CheckedReadPage` that unwraps the inner value if it exists.\n *\n * If `CheckedReadPage` returns a `std::nullopt`, **this function aborts the entire process.**\n *\n * This function should **only** be used for testing and ergonomic's sake. If it is at all possible that the buffer pool\n * manager might run out of memory, then use `CheckedPageWrite` to allow you to handle that case.\n *\n * See the documentation for `CheckedPageRead` for more information about implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param access_type The type of page access.\n * @return ReadPageGuard A page guard ensuring shared and read-only access to a page's data.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::ReadPage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> ReadPageGuard {\n  auto guard_opt = CheckedReadPage(page_id, access_type);\n\n  if (!guard_opt.has_value()) {\n    fmt::println(stderr, \"\\n`CheckedReadPage` failed to bring in page {}\\n\", page_id);\n    std::abort();\n  }\n\n  return std::move(guard_opt).value();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes a page's data out to disk unsafely.\n *\n * This function will write out a page's data to disk if it has been modified. If the given page is not in memory, this\n * function will return `false`.\n *\n * You should not take a lock on the page in this function.\n * This means that you should carefully consider when to toggle the `is_dirty_` bit.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You should probably leave implementing this function until after you have completed `CheckedReadPage` and\n * `CheckedWritePage`, as it will likely be much easier to understand what to do.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page to be flushed.\n * @return `false` if the page could not be found in the page table; otherwise, `true`.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::FlushPageUnsafe(page_id_t page_id) -> bool { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes a page's data out to disk safely.\n *\n * This function will write out a page's data to disk if it has been modified. If the given page is not in memory, this\n * function will return `false`.\n *\n * You should take a lock on the page in this function to ensure that a consistent state is flushed to disk.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You should probably leave implementing this function until after you have completed `CheckedReadPage`,\n * `CheckedWritePage`, and `Flush` in the page guards, as it will likely be much easier to understand what to do.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page to be flushed.\n * @return `false` if the page could not be found in the page table; otherwise, `true`.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::FlushPage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes all page data that is in memory to disk unsafely.\n *\n * You should not take locks on the pages in this function.\n * This means that you should carefully consider when to toggle the `is_dirty_` bit.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You should probably leave implementing this function until after you have completed `CheckedReadPage`,\n * `CheckedWritePage`, and `FlushPage`, as it will likely be much easier to understand what to do.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n */\nvoid BufferPoolManager::FlushAllPagesUnsafe() { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes all page data that is in memory to disk safely.\n *\n * You should take locks on the pages in this function to ensure that a consistent state is flushed to disk.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You should probably leave implementing this function until after you have completed `CheckedReadPage`,\n * `CheckedWritePage`, and `FlushPage`, as it will likely be much easier to understand what to do.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n */\nvoid BufferPoolManager::FlushAllPages() { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Retrieves the pin count of a page. If the page does not exist in memory, return `std::nullopt`.\n *\n * This function is thread safe. Callers may invoke this function in a multi-threaded environment where multiple threads\n * access the same page.\n *\n * This function is intended for testing purposes. If this function is implemented incorrectly, it will definitely cause\n * problems with the test suite and autograder.\n *\n * # Implementation\n *\n * We will use this function to test if your buffer pool manager is managing pin counts correctly. Since the\n * `pin_count_` field in `FrameHeader` is an atomic type, you do not need to take the latch on the frame that holds the\n * page we want to look at. Instead, you can simply use an atomic `load` to safely load the value stored. You will still\n * need to take the buffer pool latch, however.\n *\n * Again, if you are unfamiliar with atomic types, see the official C++ docs\n * [here](https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/atomic/atomic).\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to get the pin count of.\n * @return std::optional<size_t> The pin count if the page exists; otherwise, `std::nullopt`.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::GetPinCount(page_id_t page_id) -> std::optional<size_t> {\n  UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\");\n}\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n",
