Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp",
  "new_string": "void ReadPageGuard::Drop() {\n  if (!is_valid_) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Unlock the shared lock on the frame\n  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock_shared();\n\n  // Decrement pin count and potentially set evictable\n  // We need to hold the BPM latch to atomically check and update evictable status\n  {\n    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);\n    size_t old_pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1);\n    // Only set evictable if pin count is now 0 (we were the last one)\n    if (old_pin_count == 1 && frame_->pin_count_.load() == 0) {\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Invalidate the guard\n  is_valid_ = false;\n}",
  "old_string": "void ReadPageGuard::Drop() {\n  if (!is_valid_) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Unlock the shared lock on the frame\n  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock_shared();\n\n  // Decrement pin count and potentially set evictable\n  size_t old_pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1);\n  if (old_pin_count == 1) {\n    // This was the last pin, set the frame as evictable\n    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);\n    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);\n  }\n\n  // Invalidate the guard\n  is_valid_ = false;\n}",
  "replace_all": false
}

  "newString": "void ReadPageGuard::Drop() {\n  if (!is_valid_) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Unlock the shared lock on the frame\n  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock_shared();\n\n  // Decrement pin count and potentially set evictable\n  // We need to hold the BPM latch to atomically check and update evictable status\n  {\n    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);\n    size_t old_pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1);\n    // Only set evictable if pin count is now 0 (we were the last one)\n    if (old_pin_count == 1 && frame_->pin_count_.load() == 0) {\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Invalidate the guard\n  is_valid_ = false;\n}",
  "oldString": "void ReadPageGuard::Drop() {\n  if (!is_valid_) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Unlock the shared lock on the frame\n  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock_shared();\n\n  // Decrement pin count and potentially set evictable\n  size_t old_pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1);\n  if (old_pin_count == 1) {\n    // This was the last pin, set the frame as evictable\n    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);\n    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);\n  }\n\n  // Invalidate the guard\n  is_valid_ = false;\n}",
  "originalFile": "//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// page_guard.cpp\n//\n// Identification: src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#include \"storage/page/page_guard.h\"\n#include <memory>\n#include \"buffer/arc_replacer.h\"\n#include \"common/macros.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\n/**\n * @brief The only constructor for an RAII `ReadPageGuard` that creates a valid guard.\n *\n * Note that only the buffer pool manager is allowed to call this constructor.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param frame A shared pointer to the frame that holds the page we want to protect.\n * @param replacer A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's replacer.\n * @param bpm_latch A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's latch.\n * @param disk_scheduler A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's disk scheduler.\n */\nReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                             std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                             std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),\n      is_valid_(true) {\n  // The caller (BPM) should have already acquired the read lock before calling this constructor\n  // The guard takes ownership of the lock\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move constructor for `ReadPageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n */\nReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept\n    : page_id_(that.page_id_),\n      frame_(std::move(that.frame_)),\n      replacer_(std::move(that.replacer_)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(that.bpm_latch_)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(that.disk_scheduler_)),\n      is_valid_(that.is_valid_) {\n  // Invalidate the moved-from guard\n  that.is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move assignment operator for `ReadPageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each, and for the current object, make sure you release any resources it might be\n * holding on to.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n * @return ReadPageGuard& The newly valid `ReadPageGuard`.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::operator=(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept -> ReadPageGuard & {\n  if (this != &that) {\n    // First, drop any resources this guard might hold\n    Drop();\n\n    // Move the resources from 'that' to 'this'\n    page_id_ = that.page_id_;\n    frame_ = std::move(that.frame_);\n    replacer_ = std::move(that.replacer_);\n    bpm_latch_ = std::move(that.bpm_latch_);\n    disk_scheduler_ = std::move(that.disk_scheduler_);\n    is_valid_ = that.is_valid_;\n\n    // Invalidate the moved-from guard\n    that.is_valid_ = false;\n  }\n  return *this;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets the page ID of the page this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::GetPageId() const -> page_id_t {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return page_id_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a `const` pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::GetData() const -> const char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return frame_->GetData();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Returns whether the page is dirty (modified but not flushed to the disk).\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::IsDirty() const -> bool {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return frame_->is_dirty_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes this page's data safely to disk.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid ReadPageGuard::Flush() {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n\n  if (frame_->is_dirty_) {\n    // Write the page to disk\n    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n    DiskRequest request;\n    request.is_write_ = true;\n    request.data_ = frame_->GetDataMut();\n    request.page_id_ = page_id_;\n    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n    auto future = promise.get_future();\n    request.callback_ = std::move(promise);\n    requests.push_back(std::move(request));\n    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n    future.get();\n\n    frame_->is_dirty_ = false;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Manually drops a valid `ReadPageGuard`'s data. If this guard is invalid, this function does nothing.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * Make sure you don't double free! Also, think **very** **VERY** carefully about what resources you own and the order\n * in which you release those resources. If you get the ordering wrong, you will very likely fail one of the later\n * Gradescope tests. You may also want to take the buffer pool manager's latch in a very specific scenario...\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid ReadPageGuard::Drop() {\n  if (!is_valid_) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Unlock the shared lock on the frame\n  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock_shared();\n\n  // Decrement pin count and potentially set evictable\n  size_t old_pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1);\n  if (old_pin_count == 1) {\n    // This was the last pin, set the frame as evictable\n    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);\n    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);\n  }\n\n  // Invalidate the guard\n  is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/** @brief The destructor for `ReadPageGuard`. This destructor simply calls `Drop()`. */\nReadPageGuard::~ReadPageGuard() { Drop(); }\n\n/**********************************************************************************************************************/\n/**********************************************************************************************************************/\n/**********************************************************************************************************************/\n\n/**\n * @brief The only constructor for an RAII `WritePageGuard` that creates a valid guard.\n *\n * Note that only the buffer pool manager is allowed to call this constructor.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to write to.\n * @param frame A shared pointer to the frame that holds the page we want to protect.\n * @param replacer A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's replacer.\n * @param bpm_latch A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's latch.\n * @param disk_scheduler A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's disk scheduler.\n */\nWritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                               std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                               std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),\n      is_valid_(true) {\n  // The caller (BPM) should have already acquired the exclusive lock before calling this constructor\n  // The guard takes ownership of the lock\n  // Mark the frame as dirty since we're getting write access\n  frame_->is_dirty_ = true;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move constructor for `WritePageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n */\nWritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(WritePageGuard &&that) noexcept\n    : page_id_(that.page_id_),\n      frame_(std::move(that.frame_)),\n      replacer_(std::move(that.replacer_)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(that.bpm_latch_)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(that.disk_scheduler_)),\n      is_valid_(that.is_valid_) {\n  // Invalidate the moved-from guard\n  that.is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move assignment operator for `WritePageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each, and for the current object, make sure you release any resources it might be\n * holding on to.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n * @return WritePageGuard& The newly valid `WritePageGuard`.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::operator=(WritePageGuard &&that) noexcept -> WritePageGuard & {\n  if (this != &that) {\n    // First, drop any resources this guard might hold\n    Drop();\n\n    // Move the resources from 'that' to 'this'\n    page_id_ = that.page_id_;\n    frame_ = std::move(that.frame_);\n    replacer_ = std::move(that.replacer_);\n    bpm_latch_ = std::move(that.bpm_latch_);\n    disk_scheduler_ = std::move(that.disk_scheduler_);\n    is_valid_ = that.is_valid_;\n\n    // Invalidate the moved-from guard\n    that.is_valid_ = false;\n  }\n  return *this;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets the page ID of the page this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetPageId() const -> page_id_t {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return page_id_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a `const` pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetData() const -> const char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return frame_->GetData();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a mutable pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetDataMut() -> char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return frame_->GetDataMut();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Returns whether the page is dirty (modified but not flushed to the disk).\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::IsDirty() const -> bool {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return frame_->is_dirty_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes this page's data safely to disk.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid WritePageGuard::Flush() {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n\n  if (frame_->is_dirty_) {\n    // Write the page to disk\n    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n    DiskRequest request;\n    request.is_write_ = true;\n    request.data_ = frame_->GetDataMut();\n    request.page_id_ = page_id_;\n    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n    auto future = promise.get_future();\n    request.callback_ = std::move(promise);\n    requests.push_back(std::move(request));\n    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n    future.get();\n\n    frame_->is_dirty_ = false;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Manually drops a valid `WritePageGuard`'s data. If this guard is invalid, this function does nothing.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * Make sure you don't double free! Also, think **very** **VERY** carefully about what resources you own and the order\n * in which you release those resources. If you get the ordering wrong, you will very likely fail one of the later\n * Gradescope tests. You may also want to take the buffer pool manager's latch in a very specific scenario...\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid WritePageGuard::Drop() {\n  if (!is_valid_) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Unlock the exclusive lock on the frame\n  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock();\n\n  // Decrement pin count and potentially set evictable\n  size_t old_pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1);\n  if (old_pin_count == 1) {\n    // This was the last pin, set the frame as evictable\n    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);\n    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);\n  }\n\n  // Invalidate the guard\n  is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/** @brief The destructor for `WritePageGuard`. This destructor simply calls `Drop()`. */\nWritePageGuard::~WritePageGuard() { Drop(); }\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n",

* @brief The only constructor for an RAII `WritePageGuard` that creates a valid guard.\n *\n * Note that only the buffer pool manager is allowed to call this constructor.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to write to.\n * @param frame A shared pointer to the frame that holds the page we want to protect.\n * @param replacer A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's replacer.\n * @param bpm_latch A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's latch.\n * @param disk_scheduler A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's disk scheduler.\n */\nWritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                               std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                               std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),\n      is_valid_(true) {\n  // The caller (BPM) should have already acquired the exclusive lock before calling this constructor\n  // The guard takes ownership of the lock\n  // Mark the frame as dirty since we're getting write access\n  frame_->is_dirty_ = true;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move constructor for `WritePageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n */\nWritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(WritePageGuard &&that) noexcept\n    : page_id_(that.page_id_),\n      frame_(std::move(that.frame_)),\n      replacer_(std::move(that.replacer_)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(that.bpm_latch_)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(that.disk_scheduler_)),\n      is_valid_(that.is_valid_) {\n  // Invalidate the moved-from guard\n  that.is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move assignment operator for `WritePageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each, and for the current object, make sure you release any resources it might be\n * holding on to.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n * @return WritePageGuard& The newly valid `WritePageGuard`.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::operator=(WritePageGuard &&that) noexcept -> WritePageGuard & {\n  if (this != &that) {\n    // First, drop any resources this guard might hold\n    Drop();\n\n    // Move the resources from 'that' to 'this'\n    page_id_ = that.page_id_;\n    frame_ = std::move(that.frame_);\n    replacer_ = std::move(that.replacer_);\n    bpm_latch_ = std::move(that.bpm_latch_);\n    disk_scheduler_ = std::move(that.disk_scheduler_);\n    is_valid_ = that.is_valid_;\n\n    // Invalidate the moved-from guard\n    that.is_valid_ = false;\n  }\n  return *this;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets the page ID of the page this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetPageId() const -> page_id_t {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return page_id_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a `const` pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetData() const -> const char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return frame_->GetData();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a mutable pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetDataMut() -> char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return frame_->GetDataMut();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Returns whether the page is dirty (modified but not flushed to the disk).\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::IsDirty() const -> bool {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return frame_->is_dirty_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes this page's data safely to disk.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid WritePageGuard::Flush() {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n\n  if (frame_->is_dirty_) {\n    // Write the page to disk\n    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n    DiskRequest request;\n    request.is_write_ = true;\n    request.data_ = frame_->GetDataMut();\n    request.page_id_ = page_id_;\n    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n    auto future = promise.get_future();\n    request.callback_ = std::move(promise);\n    requests.push_back(std::move(request));\n    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n    future.get();\n\n    frame_->is_dirty_ = false;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Manually drops a valid `WritePageGuard`'s data. If this guard is invalid, this function does nothing.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * Make sure you don't double free! Also, think **very** **VERY** carefully about what resources you own and the order\n * in which you release those resources. If you get the ordering wrong, you will very likely fail one of the later\n * Gradescope tests. You may also want to take the buffer pool manager's latch in a very specific scenario...\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid WritePageGuard::Drop() {\n  if (!is_valid_) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Unlock the exclusive lock on the frame\n  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock();\n\n  // Decrement pin count and potentially set evictable\n  size_t old_pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1);\n  if (old_pin_count == 1) {\n    // This was the last pin, set the frame as evictable\n    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);\n    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);\n  }\n\n  // Invalidate the guard\n  is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/** @brief The destructor for `WritePageGuard`. This destructor simply calls `Drop()`. */\nWritePageGuard::~WritePageGuard() { Drop(); }\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "   frame_->rwlatch_.unlock();",
        " ",
        "   // Decrement pin count and potentially set evictable",
        "-  size_t old_pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1);",
        "-  if (old_pin_count == 1) {",
        "-    // This was the last pin, set the frame as evictable",
        "+  // We need to hold the BPM latch to atomically check and update evictable status",
        "+  {",
        "     std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);",
        "-    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);",
        "+    size_t old_pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1);",
        "+    // Only set evictable if pin count is now 0 (we were the last one)",
        "+    if (old_pin_count == 1 && frame_->pin_count_.load() == 0) {",
        "+      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);",
        "+    }",
        "   }",
        " ",
        "   // Invalidate the guard"

1. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp
Before
/**
 * TODO(P1): Add implementation
 *
 * @brief Performs the Replace operation as described by the writeup
 * that evicts from either mfu_ or mru_ according to balancing policy.
 *
 * If you wish to refer to the original ARC paper, please note that there are
 * two changes in our implementation:
 * 1. When the size of mru_ equals the target size, we don't check
 * the last access as the paper did when deciding which list to evict from.
 * This is fine since the original decision is stated to be arbitrary.
 * 2. Entries that are not evictable are skipped. If all entries from the desired side
 * (mru_ / mfu_) are pinned, we instead try victimize the other side (mfu_ / mru_),
 * and move it to its corresponding ghost list (mfu_ghost_ / mru_ghost_).
 *
 * @return frame id of the evicted frame, or std::nullopt if cannot evict
 */
auto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> { return std::nullopt; }
After
/**
 * TODO(P1): Add implementation
 *
 * @brief Performs the Replace operation as described by the writeup
 * that evicts from either mfu_ or mru_ according to balancing policy.
 *
 * If you wish to refer to the original ARC paper, please note that there are
 * two changes in our implementation:
 * 1. When the size of mru_ equals the target size, we don't check
 * the last access as the paper did when deciding which list to evict from.
 * This is fine since the original decision is stated to be arbitrary.
 * 2. Entries that are not evictable are skipped. If all entries from the desired side
 * (mru_ / mfu_) are pinned, we instead try victimize the other side (mfu_ / mru_),
 * and move it to its corresponding ghost list (mfu_ghost_ / mru_ghost_).
 *
 * @return frame id of the evicted frame, or std::nullopt if cannot evict
 */
auto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {
  std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> lock(latch_);

  if (curr_size_ == 0) {
    return std::nullopt;
  }

  // Helper lambda to find and evict from a list
  auto evict_from_list = [&](std::list<frame_id_t> &list,
                             std::list<page_id_t> &ghost_list) -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {
    for (auto it = list.begin(); it != list.end(); ++it) {
      frame_id_t fid = *it;
      auto status_it = alive_map_.find(fid);
      if (status_it != alive_map_.end() && status_it->second->evictable_) {
        // Found an evictable frame
        auto status = status_it->second;
        page_id_t pid = status->page_id_;

        // Remove from current list
        list.erase(it);

        // Add to ghost list
        status->arc_status_ =
            (&list == &mru_) ? ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST : ArcStatus::MFU_GHOST;
        ghost_list.push_back(pid);
        ghost_map_[pid] = status;

        /
... (truncated)
2. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp
Before
auto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> { return std::nullopt; }
After
auto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {
  std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> lock(latch_);

  if (curr_size_ == 0) {
    return std::nullopt;
  }

  // Helper lambda to find and evict from a list
  auto evict_from_list = [&](std::list<frame_id_t> &list,
                             std::list<page_id_t> &ghost_list) -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {
    for (auto it = list.begin(); it != list.end(); ++it) {
      frame_id_t fid = *it;
      auto status_it = alive_map_.find(fid);
      if (status_it != alive_map_.end() && status_it->second->evictable_) {
        // Found an evictable frame
        auto status = status_it->second;
        page_id_t pid = status->page_id_;

        // Remove from current list
        list.erase(it);

        // Add to ghost list
        status->arc_status_ =
            (&list == &mru_) ? ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST : ArcStatus::MFU_GHOST;
        ghost_list.push_back(pid);
        ghost_map_[pid] = status;

        // Remove from alive map
        alive_map_.erase(fid);
        curr_size_--;

        return fid;
      }
    }
    return std::nullopt;
  };

  // Determine which list to evict from based on mru_target_size_
  // If mru_.size() >= mru_target_size_, evict from mru_; otherwise evict from mfu_
  bool try_mru_first = mru_.size() >= mru_target_size_;

  std::optional<frame_id_t> result;
  if (try_mru_first) {
    result = evict_from_list(mru_, mru_ghost_);
    if (!result.has_value()) {
      // All entries in mru_ are pinned, try mfu_
      result = evict_from_list(mfu_, mfu_ghost_);
    }
  } else {
    result = evict_from_list(mfu_, mfu_ghost_);
    if (!result.has_value()) {
      // All entries in mfu_ are pinned, try mru_
      result = evict_from_list(mru_, mru_ghost_);
    }
  }

  return result;
}
3. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp
Before
void ArcReplacer::RecordAccess(frame_id_t frame_id, page_id_t page_id, [[maybe_unused]] AccessType access_type) {}
After
void ArcReplacer::RecordAccess(frame_id_t frame_id, page_id_t page_id, [[maybe_unused]] AccessType access_type) {
  std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> lock(latch_);

  // Case 1: Page is already in mru_ or mfu_ (alive)
  auto alive_it = alive_map_.find(frame_id);
  if (alive_it != alive_map_.end()) {
    auto status = alive_it->second;
    // Move to front of mfu_
    if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU) {
      // Find and remove from mru_
      mru_.remove(frame_id);
    } else if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MFU) {
      // Find and remove from mfu_
      mfu_.remove(frame_id);
    }
    // Add to front of mfu_ (end of list = most recent)
    mfu_.push_back(frame_id);
    status->arc_status_ = ArcStatus::MFU;
    return;
  }

  // Case 2: Hit on mru_ghost_
  auto mru_ghost_it = ghost_map_.find(page_id);
  if (mru_ghost_it != ghost_map_.end() && mru_ghost_it->second->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST) {
    // Increase target size: p = min(c, p + max(|mfu_ghost_| / |mru_ghost_|, 1))
    size_t delta = std::max(mfu_ghost_.size() / std::max(mru_ghost_.size(), size_t{1}), size_t{1});
    mru_target_size_ = std::min(replacer_size_, mru_target_size_ + delta);

    // Remove from mru_ghost_
    mru_ghost_.remove(page_id);
    ghost_map_.erase(page_id);

    // Create new entry in mfu_
    auto status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, frame_id, false, ArcStatus::MFU);
    mfu_.push_back(frame_id);
    alive_map_[frame_id] = status;
    return;
  }

  // Case 3: Hit on mfu_ghost_
  if (mru_ghost_it != ghost_map_.end() && mru_ghost_it->second->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MFU_GHOST) {
    // Decrease target size: p = max(0, p - max(|mru_ghost_| / |mfu_ghost_|, 1))
    size_t delta = std::max(mru_ghost_.size() / std::max(mfu_ghost_.size(), size_t{1}), size_t{1});
  
... (truncated)
4. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp
Before
void ArcReplacer::SetEvictable(frame_id_t frame_id, bool set_evictable) {}
After
void ArcReplacer::SetEvictable(frame_id_t frame_id, bool set_evictable) {
  std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> lock(latch_);

  auto it = alive_map_.find(frame_id);
  if (it == alive_map_.end()) {
    // Frame not found - this shouldn't happen but silently return
    return;
  }

  auto status = it->second;
  if (status->evictable_ == set_evictable) {
    // No change needed
    return;
  }

  status->evictable_ = set_evictable;
  if (set_evictable) {
    curr_size_++;
  } else {
    curr_size_--;
  }
}
5. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp
Before
void ArcReplacer::Remove(frame_id_t frame_id) {}
After
void ArcReplacer::Remove(frame_id_t frame_id) {
  std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> lock(latch_);

  auto it = alive_map_.find(frame_id);
  if (it == alive_map_.end()) {
    // Frame not found - just return
    return;
  }

  auto status = it->second;
  if (!status->evictable_) {
    // Cannot remove a non-evictable frame
    BUSTUB_ENSURE(false, "Remove called on non-evictable frame");
    return;
  }

  // Remove from appropriate list
  if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU) {
    mru_.remove(frame_id);
  } else if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MFU) {
    mfu_.remove(frame_id);
  }

  // Update size and remove from alive_map
  curr_size_--;
  alive_map_.erase(frame_id);
}
6. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp
Before
auto ArcReplacer::Size() -> size_t { return 0; }
After
auto ArcReplacer::Size() -> size_t {
  std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> lock(latch_);
  return curr_size_;
}

Before

  // First, check if we need to make room in ghost lists (case 4A or 4B)
  size_t total_alive = mru_.size() + mfu_.size();
  size_t mru_with_ghost = mru_.size() + mru_ghost_.size();
After
  // Case 4: Miss (not in any list) - add to mru_
  // First, check if we need to make room in ghost lists (case 4A or 4B)
  size_t mru_with_ghost = mru_.size() + mru_ghost_.size();
8. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp
Before
auto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {
  std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> lock(latch_);

  if (curr_size_ == 0) {
    return std::nullopt;
  }

  // Helper lambda to find and evict from a list
  auto evict_from_list = [&](std::list<frame_id_t> &list,
                             std::list<page_id_t> &ghost_list) -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {
    for (auto it = list.begin(); it != list.end(); ++it) {
      frame_id_t fid = *it;
      auto status_it = alive_map_.find(fid);
      if (status_it != alive_map_.end() && status_it->second->evictable_) {
        // Found an evictable frame
        auto status = status_it->second;
        page_id_t pid = status->page_id_;

        // Remove from current list
        list.erase(it);

        // Add to ghost list
        status->arc_status_ =
            (&list == &mru_) ? ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST : ArcStatus::MFU_GHOST;
        ghost_list.push_back(pid);
        ghost_map_[pid] = status;

        // Remove from alive map
        alive_map_.erase(fid);
        curr_size_--;

        return fid;
      }
    }
    return std::nullopt;
  };

  // Determine which list to evict from based on mru_target_size_
  // If mru_.size() >= mru_target_size_, evict from mru_; otherwise evict from mfu_
  bool try_mru_first = mru_.size() >= mru_target_size_;

  std::optional<frame_id_t> result;
  if (try_mru_first) {
    result = evict_from_list(mru_, mru_ghost_);
    if (!result.has_value()) {
      // All entries in mru_ are pinned, try mfu_
      result = evict_from_list(mfu_, mfu_ghost_);
    }
  } else {
    result = evict_from_list(mfu_, mfu_ghost_);
    if (!result.has_value()) {
      // All entries in mfu_ are pinned, try mru_
      result = evict_from_list(mru_, mru_ghost_);
    }
  }

  return result;
}
After

                             std::list<page_id_t> &ghost_list,

        // Found an evictable frame

        // Remove from current list
        list.erase(it);

        // Add to ghost list
        status->arc_status_ = ghost_status;

        alive_map_.erase(fid);

  std::optional<frame_id_t> result;
  if (try_mru_first) {
    result = evict_from_list(mru_, mru_ghost_, ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST);
    if (!result.has_value()) {
      // All entries in mru_ are pinned, try mfu_

Before
ReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,
                             std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,
                             std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)
    : page_id_(page_id),
      frame_(std::move(frame)),
      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),
      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),
      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)) {
  UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation.");
}
After
ReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,
                             std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,
                             std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)
    : page_id_(page_id),
      frame_(std::move(frame)),
      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),
      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),
      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),
      is_valid_(true) {
  // The caller (BPM) should have already acquired the read lock before calling this constructor
  // The guard takes ownership of the lock
}
10. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp
Before
ReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept {}
After
ReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept
    : page_id_(that.page_id_),
      frame_(std::move(that.frame_)),
      replacer_(std::move(that.replacer_)),
      bpm_latch_(std::move(that.bpm_latch_)),
      disk_scheduler_(std::move(that.disk_scheduler_)),
      is_valid_(that.is_valid_) {
  // Invalidate the moved-from guard
  that.is_valid_ = false;
}
11. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp
Before
auto ReadPageGuard::operator=(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept -> ReadPageGuard & { return *this; }
After
auto ReadPageGuard::operator=(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept -> ReadPageGuard & {
  if (this != &that) {
    // First, drop any resources this guard might hold
    Drop();

    // Move the resources from 'that' to 'this'
    page_id_ = that.page_id_;
    frame_ = std::move(that.frame_);
    replacer_ = std::move(that.replacer_);
    bpm_latch_ = std::move(that.bpm_latch_);
    disk_scheduler_ = std::move(that.disk_scheduler_);
    is_valid_ = that.is_valid_;

    // Invalidate the moved-from guard
    that.is_valid_ = false;
  }
  return *this;
}
12. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp
Before
void ReadPageGuard::Flush() { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }
After
void ReadPageGuard::Flush() {
  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, "tried to use an invalid read guard");

  if (frame_->is_dirty_) {
    // Write the page to disk
    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;
    DiskRequest request;
    request.is_write_ = true;
    request.data_ = frame_->GetDataMut();
    request.page_id_ = page_id_;
    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();
    auto future = promise.get_future();
    request.callback_ = std::move(promise);
    requests.push_back(std::move(request));
    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);
    future.get();

    frame_->is_dirty_ = false;
  }
}
13. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp
Before
void ReadPageGuard::Drop() { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }
After
void ReadPageGuard::Drop() {
  if (!is_valid_) {
    return;
  }

  // Unlock the shared lock on the frame
  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock_shared();

  // Decrement pin count and potentially set evictable
  size_t old_pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1);
  if (old_pin_count == 1) {
    // This was the last pin, set the frame as evictable
    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);
    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);
  }

  // Invalidate the guard
  is_valid_ = false;
}

Before
WritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,
                               std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,
                               std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)
    : page_id_(page_id),
      frame_(std::move(frame)),
      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),
      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),
      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)) {
  UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation.");
}
After
WritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,
                               std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,
                               std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)
    : page_id_(page_id),
      frame_(std::move(frame)),
      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),
      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),
      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),
      is_valid_(true) {
  // The caller (BPM) should have already acquired the exclusive lock before calling this constructor
  // The guard takes ownership of the lock
  // Mark the frame as dirty since we're getting write access
  frame_->is_dirty_ = true;
}

void WritePageGuard::Drop() {
  if (!is_valid_) {
    return;
  }

  // Unlock the exclusive lock on the frame
  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock();

  // Decrement pin count and potentially set evictable
  size_t old_pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1);
  if (old_pin_count == 1) {
    // This was the last pin, set the frame as evictable
    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);
    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);
  }

  // Invalidate the guard
  is_valid_ = false;
}

auto BufferPoolManager::NewPage() -> page_id_t {
  // Atomically increment and return the previous value as the new page ID
  return next_page_id_.fetch_add(1);

After
auto BufferPoolManager::CheckedWritePage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<WritePageGuard> {
  std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame;
  frame_id_t frame_id;

  {
    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);

    // Case 1: Page is already in the buffer pool
    auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);
    if (it != page_table_.end()) {
      frame_id = it->second;
      frame = frames_[frame_id];

      // Increment pin count
      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);

      // Mark as not evictable
      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);

      // Record access for replacement policy
      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);
    } else {
      // Case 2: Need to bring page into memory
      // Try to find a free frame first
      if (!free_frames_.empty()) {
        frame_id = free_frames_.front();
        free_frames_.pop_front();
        frame = frames_[frame_id];
      } else {
        // Case 3: Need to evict a frame
        auto evicted_frame_id = replacer_->Evict();
        if (!evicted_frame_id.has_value()) {
          // No frames can be evicted
          return std::nullopt;
        }
        frame_id = evicted_frame_id.value();
        frame = frames_[frame_id];

        // Find the page that was in this frame and remove it from page_table_
        for (auto pt_it = page_table_.begin(); pt_it != page_table_.end(); ++pt_it) {
          if (pt_it->second == frame_id) {
            // If the page is dirty, write it to disk first
            if (frame->is_dirty_) {
              std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;
              DiskRequest req;
              req.is_write_ = true;
              req.data_ = frame->GetDataMut();

              auto promise = disk_scheduler_->

22. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp
Before
auto BufferPoolManager::DeletePage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }
After
auto BufferPoolManager::DeletePage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool {
  std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);

  // Check if page is in the buffer pool
  auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);
  if (it == page_table_.end()) {
    // Page is not in memory - deallocate from disk
    disk_scheduler_->DeallocatePage(page_id);
    return true;
  }

  frame_id_t frame_id = it->second;
  auto frame = frames_[frame_id];

  // Check if the page is pinned
  if (frame->pin_count_.load() > 0) {
    // Cannot delete a pinned page
    return false;
  }

  // Remove from page table
  page_table_.erase(it);

  // Remove from replacer
  replacer_->Remove(frame_id);

  // Reset the frame
  frame->Reset();

  // Add frame back to free list
  free_frames_.push_back(frame_id);

  // Deallocate the page from disk
  disk_scheduler_->DeallocatePage(page_id);

  return true;
}
23. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp
Before
auto BufferPoolManager::FlushPageUnsafe(page_id_t page_id) -> bool { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }
After
auto BufferPoolManager::FlushPageUnsafe(page_id_t page_id) -> bool {
  std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);

  // Check if page is in the buffer pool
  auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);
  if (it == page_table_.end()) {
    return false;
  }

  frame_id_t frame_id = it->second;
  auto frame = frames_[frame_id];

  // Write the page to disk if dirty
  if (frame->is_dirty_) {
    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;
    DiskRequest req;
    req.is_write_ = true;
    req.data_ = frame->GetDataMut();
    req.page_id_ = page_id;
    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();
    auto future = promise.get_future();
    req.callback_ = std::move(promise);
    requests.push_back(std::move(req));
    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);
    future.get();

    frame->is_dirty_ = false;
  }

  return true;
}
24. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp
Before
auto BufferPoolManager::FlushPage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }
After
auto BufferPoolManager::FlushPage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool {
  std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame;

  {
    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);

    // Check if page is in the buffer pool
    auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);
    if (it == page_table_.end()) {
      return false;
    }

    frame_id_t frame_id = it->second;
    frame = frames_[frame_id];
  }

  // Take shared lock to ensure consistent state
  std::shared_lock<std::shared_mutex> lock(frame->rwlatch_);

  // Write the page to disk if dirty
  if (frame->is_dirty_) {
    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;
    DiskRequest req;
    req.is_write_ = true;
    req.data_ = frame->GetDataMut();
    req.page_id_ = page_id;
    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();
    auto future = promise.get_future();
    req.callback_ = std::move(promise);
    requests.push_back(std::move(req));
    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);
    future.get();

    frame->is_dirty_ = false;
  }

  return true;
}
25. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp
Before
void BufferPoolManager::FlushAllPagesUnsafe() { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }
After
void BufferPoolManager::FlushAllPagesUnsafe() {
  std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);

  for (const auto &[page_id, frame_id] : page_table_) {
    auto frame = frames_[frame_id];
    if (frame->is_dirty_) {
      std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;
      DiskRequest req;
      req.is_write_ = true;
      req.data_ = frame->GetDataMut();
      req.page_id_ = page_id;
      auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();
      auto future = promise.get_future();
      req.callback_ = std::move(promise);
      requests.push_back(std::move(req));
      disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);
      future.get();

      frame->is_dirty_ = false;
    }
  }
}
26. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp
Before
void BufferPoolManager::FlushAllPages() { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }
After
void BufferPoolManager::FlushAllPages() {
  std::vector<std::pair<page_id_t, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader>>> pages_to_flush;

  {
    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);
    for (const auto &[page_id, frame_id] : page_table_) {
      pages_to_flush.emplace_back(page_id, frames_[frame_id]);
    }
  }

  for (const auto &[page_id, frame] : pages_to_flush) {
    std::shared_lock<std::shared_mutex> lock(frame->rwlatch_);
    if (frame->is_dirty_) {
      std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;
      DiskRequest req;
      req.is_write_ = true;
      req.data_ = frame->GetDataMut();
      req.page_id_ = page_id;
      auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();
      auto future = promise.get_future();
      req.callback_ = std::move(promise);
      requests.push_back(std::move(req));
      disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);
      future.get();

      frame->is_dirty_ = false;
    }
  }
}
27. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp
Before
auto BufferPoolManager::GetPinCount(page_id_t page_id) -> std::optional<size_t> {
  UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation.");
}
After
auto BufferPoolManager::GetPinCount(page_id_t page_id) -> std::optional<size_t> {
  std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);

  // Check if page is in the buffer pool
  auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);
  if (it == page_table_.end()) {
    return std::nullopt;
  }

  frame_id_t frame_id = it->second;
  auto frame = frames_[frame_id];

  return frame->pin_count_.load();
}

DiskScheduler::DiskScheduler(DiskManager *disk_manager) : disk_manager_(disk_manager) {
  UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation.");
  // Spawn the background thread
  background_thread_.emplace([&] { StartWorkerThread(); });
}
After
DiskScheduler::DiskScheduler(DiskManager *disk_manager) : disk_manager_(disk_manager) {
  // Spawn the background thread
  background_thread_.emplace([&] { StartWorkerThread(); });
}
29. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/disk/disk_scheduler.cpp
Before
void DiskScheduler::Schedule(std::vector<DiskRequest> &requests) {}
After
void DiskScheduler::Schedule(std::vector<DiskRequest> &requests) {
  for (auto &req : requests) {
    request_queue_.Put(std::move(req));
  }
}
30. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/disk/disk_scheduler.cpp
Before
void DiskScheduler::StartWorkerThread() {}
After
void DiskScheduler::StartWorkerThread() {
  while (true) {
    auto request_opt = request_queue_.Get();
    if (!request_opt.has_value()) {
      // std::nullopt signals that we should exit
      break;
    }

    DiskRequest &request = request_opt.value();
    if (request.is_write_) {
      disk_manager_->WritePage(request.page_id_, request.data_);
    } else {
      disk_manager_->ReadPage(request.page_id_, request.data_);
    }

    // Signal completion
    request.callback_.set_value(true);
  }
}
31. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp
Before
void ReadPageGuard::Drop() {
  if (!is_valid_) {
    return;
  }

  // Unlock the shared lock on the frame
  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock_shared();

  // Decrement pin count and potentially set evictable
  size_t old_pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1);
  if (old_pin_count == 1) {
    // This was the last pin, set the frame as evictable
    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);
    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);
  }

  // Invalidate the guard
  is_valid_ = false;
}
After
void ReadPageGuard::Drop() {
  if (!is_valid_) {
    return;
  }

  // Unlock the shared lock on the frame
  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock_shared();

  // Decrement pin count and potentially set evictable
  // We need to hold the BPM latch to check the final pin count safely
  {
    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);
    size_t old_pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1);
    if (old_pin_count == 1) {
      // Check again that pin_count is actually 0 (might have been re-pinned)
      if (frame_->pin_count_.load() == 0) {
        replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);
      }
    }
  }

  // Invalidate the guard
  is_valid_ = false;
}
32. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp
Before
void ReadPageGuard::Drop() {
  if (!is_valid_) {
    return;
  }

  // Unlock the shared lock on the frame
  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock_shared();

  // Decrement pin count and potentially set evictable
  size_t old_pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1);
  if (old_pin_count == 1) {
    // This was the last pin, set the frame as evictable
    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);
    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);
  }

  // Invalidate the guard
  is_valid_ = false;
}
After
void ReadPageGuard::Drop() {
  if (!is_valid_) {
    return;
  }

  // Unlock the shared lock on the frame
  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock_shared();

  // Decrement pin count and potentially set evictable
  // We need to hold the BPM latch to atomically check and update evictable status
  {
    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);
    size_t old_pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1);
    // Only set evictable if pin count is now 0 (we were the last one)
    if (old_pin_count == 1 && frame_->pin_count_.load() == 0) {
      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);
    }
  }

  // Invalidate the guard
  is_valid_ = false;
}
33. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp
Before
void WritePageGuard::Drop() {
  if (!is_valid_) {
    return;
  }

  // Unlock the exclusive lock on the frame
  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock();

  // Decrement pin count and potentially set evictable
  size_t old_pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1);
  if (old_pin_count == 1) {
    // This was the last pin, set the frame as evictable
    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);
    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);
  }

  // Invalidate the guard
  is_valid_ = false;
}
After
void WritePageGuard::Drop() {
  if (!is_valid_) {
    return;
  }

  // Unlock the exclusive lock on the frame
  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock();

  // Decrement pin count and potentially set evictable
  // We need to hold the BPM latch to atomically check and update evictable status
  {
    std::scoped_lock<std::mutex> latch(*bpm_latch_);
    size_t old_pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1);
    // Only set evictable if pin count is now 0 (we were the last one)
    if (old_pin_count == 1 && frame_->pin_count_.load() == 0) {
      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);
    }
  }

  // Invalidate the guard
  is_valid_ = false;
}

  echo "Running $test..."
  ASAN_OPTIONS=detect_leaks=0 ./test/buffer_pool_manager_test --gtest_also_run_disabled_tests --gtest_filter="BufferPoolManagerTest.DISABLED_$test" 2>&1 | tail -3
  echo ""
done
